The air expelling from the reporters' lungs seemed to express the death throes of the notion that Trump had come to power due to his corruption rather than the establishment's failure to provide for the people it claimed to serve. | | ---|---|--- The tension in the cramped space that once served as the White House swimming pool was palpable, having built up after nearly a decade of hysterical reporting about "Russian Collusion" known alternatively as "The Russian Hoax." It seemed fitting that the hard truth should manifest itself in a place that had once been where presidents past found respite through aquatic escape: All the old traditions of civility and impartial reporting had drained from the White House grounds along with the water in the pool. DNI Gabbard had just released a trove of declassified documents indicating what many people--at least those who can see through the bullshit--already knew: Donald Trump did not collude with the Russian government to steal the 2016 election from Hillary Clinton, and the intelligence assessment after the election would have reflected that truth were it not for scurrilous meddling from Barack Obama and his spy bosses. I know firsthand how real world events can be twisted and manipulated to serve a politically expedient narrative. I saw as much during the 2020 shooting in Kenosha and then the subsequent trial of Kyle Rittenhouse and when I was on the ground amidst the mob that stormed into the Capitol on January 6th. What I learned from those experiences is that it's important to go to the source material rather than take the word of some eggheads in a studio who get paid to tell people what they want to hear, and not the uncomfortable truth. A message from our sponsor (piece continues below) | | ---|---|--- Want to know what's really in that $12 bottle at the store? Chemical dyes Fake oak flavoring Grape concentrate These mass-market wines are made in factories owned by global corporations. What they don't want you drinking? Wine made in tiny batches by honest, hardworking families -- deep in the Andes, or tucked away in the hills of Tuscany. Every quarter, we ship 6 rare bottles -- from places so remote, most Americans will never even hear about them. These wines aren't for sale in stores, and they'll never be featured on a celebrity's Instagram. They're for people like you. Folks who still believe in hard work, tradition, and doing things the right way. You won't find these wines at Costco. And that's a good thing. - Will Bonner (Piece continues) In this case the raw evidence was the classified documents that DNI Gabbard released, and the uncomfortable truth was that Donald Trump was elected, not because he was a Russian agent, but because the "Hope and Change" that Obama promised amounted to more bailouts for Wall Street and a perpetuation of the forever wars that he vowed to end in the Middle East. Unable to fathom the fact that Trump was elected largely due to his own failures, Obama and his lackeys sought to spin a narrative that Trump had actually won because he was colluding with Vlad Putin, and not because he had capitalized on the disillusionment that stemmed from Obama's failure to change anything that improved the lives of the average Americans he pledged to save. Gabbard laid out a presentation of declassified evidence to address two main questions: did Russia want Trump to win the election, and did Trump collude with Russia to achieve that aim? According to DNI Gabbard and the declassified documents her office recently released, the answer to both those questions is no. Gabbard claimed that Obama and his intel officials knew these answers, yet sought to undermine then President-elect Trump anyway. "There is irrefutable evidence that details how President Obama and his national security team directed the creation of an intelligence community assessment that they knew was false. They knew it would promote this contrived narrative that Russia interfered in the 2016 election to help President Trump win, selling it to the American people as though it were true. It wasn't." One of the key claims in this report stated, "One scant, unclear, and unverifiable fragment of a sentence from one of the substandard reports constitutes the only classified information cited to suggest Putin 'aspired' to help Trump win." It goes on to identify the source of this claim that Putin wanted Trump to win: "[REDACTED] CIA operations officers declined to publish the report when it was acquired [REDACTED] in February 2016, considering it 'odd' and 'lacking authoritativeness.'" While the unnamed CIA officers declined to publish the report in February--ten months ahead of Trump's 2016 win--the recently declassified document alleges that--a month after Trump's win--the shoddy intelligence was included in a "full review" published by the then-Director of the CIA, John Brennan. "It was only disseminated in December 2016, on DCIA's post-election 'full review' order to put out previously unpublished information, and experienced CIA officers said that it 'would not have met the threshold' for dissemination otherwise." The document also points to multiple more credible intelligence reports that conflicted with the assessment that Putin wanted Trump to win, yet were excluded from the findings of the Intelligence Community in 2016, "Putin told him [a close confidant] he did not care who won the election." Furthermore, a December 8 Presidential Daily Briefing set to be delivered to both President Barack Obama and President-Elect Trump stated that Russian meddling did not impact the vote counts of the election and had been withheld from delivery. Presumably this was at the direction of then-President Obama because the next day Obama held a secret briefing with his spy chiefs, after which DNI Clapper's assistant sent an email to intelligence leaders with the subject line "POTUS tasking on Russia Election Meddling," asking the top brass to generate a new "assessment per the President's request." So one month after the election, the intel community assessed that Putin did NOT interfere with the US election with the goal of getting Trump to win and yet our country was put through a multi-year charade in which Obama, Clinton, Clapper, Comey, Brennan, and their brainless allies in the media claimed that there was evidence that Putin not only wanted Trump to win, but that Trump had also colluded with Putin to achieve this aim. Moreover, the document claims that portions of the now-debunked "Steele dossier" were used in an effort to make the Trump "Russian Collusion" narrative stick. An Inspector General investigation found that the "Steele dossier" was used to gain secret FISA warrants for members of the Trump campaign and that FBI agents continued to pursue those warrants even after they knew the findings of the dossier were demonstrably false. For all those who have claimed that there is no "deep state" and that Trump is a "Russian puppet," perhaps for once in their shameless careers they should try taking a walk. Doubtful that they will, I have good intelligence indicating that the longer they cling to these outdated lies, the less relevant they will become. As for Obama and the spy bosses that were responsible for weaving this false narrative from whole cloth, they may not see jail time, but history will show that they are lying, forked-tongued snakes. Friend, In the past two months, Israeli and U.S. forces have killed more than 1,000 starving Palestinians who were waiting for food at U.S.-backed “aid sites” in Gaza. The genocide has gotten worse as the Israeli government continues to block months’ worth of food and aid that’s waiting right over the border. Children are dying every day in Gaza from severe malnutrition. Eighteen of my colleagues in Congress joined me in a letter demanding the U.S. end its complicity in this forced starvation. But most of my colleagues continue to engage in genocide denial while voting to send U.S. taxpayer dollars to help Israel’s military kill more children. **Recently, the House of Representatives passed a military spending package that included $600 million for Israel’s military. Only three Democrats joined me in voting to block that funding: Representatives Ilhan Omar, Summer Lee, and Al Green.** Most of our colleagues take money from AIPAC, so our voices are needed in Congress more than ever. But their courageous vote has made them a target, which could be used against them in upcoming elections. **So I want to help raise $100,000 for their re-election campaigns.** [**Will you rush a donation to keep pro-Palestinian lawmakers Ilhan Omar, Summer Lee, and Al Green in Congress? Thank you. Together, we’re standing with the majority of Americans who want to stop funding the genocide in Gaza. Seven out of 10 Democrats support an arms embargo, and there’s overwhelming bipartisan support for holding Israel’s government accountable for its war crimes.
Across the country and the world, everyday people are taking action in solidarity with Palestinians. They’re hunger striking, banging pots and pans, marching, blocking weapons shipments, and demanding their elected representatives take action to stop the blockade and stop the genocide.
It’s time for our elected leaders to listen to the people. This genocide cannot continue without U.S. support, so we need as many U.S. lawmakers as possible to stop it.
[**If you can, please split a contribution today between four Congressmembers who will keep fighting to end the genocide of the Palestinian people: Ilhan Omar, Summer Lee, Al Green, and me.**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqsXu0R1XDWRVJnEMC9_ePsJoYLO7imVk-0eAE_R-0L9oegu06-RwrkQskRGRpHvGZclEgy1keHlrCay58a0X3vD1TIOGw3PikDtViPfIaMu4b3fDWSaPyLMDgA8lACfa4G7F9Q6FCEuQ3qkhjMI41UFntCicEyQ_lsnYwdBGtYc8Nlefkdy2O0OSyvfirMqBWaIjWhC8L-GPDWHAiQLFG1zd7kT0D1P_za94Xm2DqSK4ot9Dw9W549hg5J73fyxyDtz7N0kBINXToJ40swbVd34YAAMAwIDxGVdABJrZ6etGtTIHLZhapuHRivB4q4Cl5NP6RZtnMwiE4fCdDROYgGBYkLZvOJLShRl2xD1JGlAJIEMXAiKNIIaGdiuy_EzL6yq8v5FEn_EMJi0kSBuZ7Atu3GVXNWqQnzm2TsiZsNj2jrWZTJimJyDiwWG_wHT_vM/4ij/F8u7JnOxSYyfRcTMnfw1Kw/h8/h001.qfMv0CvY9HgGdSSMK8BP0BtWMyraN82mTK6uVxz4ksI)

Together, we are building a world where all people can live in freedom, with dignity and safety.
In solidarity,
Rashida

From: **Rashida Tlaib******

Subject: My colleagues’ courageous vote made them the target of pro-genocide groups

Friend,
My colleagues and I are standing with doctors, veterans, over 100 humanitarian groups, and everyday people across the world who are demanding Israel’s government end the forced starvation of 2 million Palestinians in Gaza.
Palestinian children are now dying daily from malnutrition, even though months’ worth of food is waiting right outside Gaza’s border. The UN has estimated that at least 14,000 infants could die if the blockade on Gaza continues.
Two months ago, the U.S. government replaced Gaza’s humanitarian aid system with what the UN has called “death traps.” At these deadly “aid sites,” U.S. and Israeli forces have shot and killed more than 1,000 starving Palestinians trying to get food.
We’re making noise in Congress to end this escalating genocide of my people. But **most of my colleagues in Congress are still denying the genocide, while propping it up with their votes.**
The House of Representatives recently passed another massive military funding bill, with $600 million U.S. taxpayer dollars for Israel’s military.**Four Democrats voted for an amendment to stop funding from going to the apartheid government of Israel: Summer Lee, Ilhan Omar, Al Green, and me.**
We know their courageous vote has made them a target by pro-genocide groups who want the killings and starvation to continue.

clean_body: Three Democrats voted with me to stop funding from going to Israel’s government: Ilhan Omar, Summer Lee, and Al Green. | | | Friend, In the past two months, Israeli and U.S. forces have killed more than 1,000 starving Palestinians who were waiting for food at U.S.-backed “aid sites” in Gaza. The genocide has gotten worse as the Israeli government continues to block months’ worth of food and aid that’s waiting right over the border. Children are dying every day in Gaza from severe malnutrition. Eighteen of my colleagues in Congress joined me in a letter demanding the U.S. end its complicity in this forced starvation. But most of my colleagues continue to engage in genocide denial while voting to send U.S. taxpayer dollars to help Israel’s military kill more children. Recently, the House of Representatives passed a military spending package that included $600 million for Israel’s military. Only three Democrats joined me in voting to block that funding: Representatives Ilhan Omar, Summer Lee, and Al Green. Most of our colleagues take money from AIPAC, so our voices are needed in Congress more than ever. But their courageous vote has made them a target, which could be used against them in upcoming elections. So I want to help raise $100,000 for their re-election campaigns. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your secure donation will go through immediately, split evenly between Al Green, Ilhan Omar, Summer Lee, and Rashida Tlaib | | | | | | | | | | Thank you. Together, we’re standing with the majority of Americans who want to stop funding the genocide in Gaza. Seven out of 10 Democrats support an arms embargo, and there’s overwhelming bipartisan support for holding Israel’s government accountable for its war crimes. Across the country and the world, everyday people are taking action in solidarity with Palestinians. They’re hunger striking, banging pots and pans, marching, blocking weapons shipments, and demanding their elected representatives take action to stop the blockade and stop the genocide. It’s time for our elected leaders to listen to the people. This genocide cannot continue without U.S. support, so we need as many U.S. lawmakers as possible to stop it. Thank you so much for your support. However you can, please keep speaking up for Palestine and for Gaza. We must continue pushing back against the racist dehumanization that normalizes dead Palestinians. Together, we are building a world where all people can live in freedom, with dignity and safety. In solidarity, Rashida \---------- Forwarded message --------- From: Rashida Tlaib** Date: Sat, Jul 26, 2025 Subject: My colleagues’ courageous vote made them the target of pro-genocide groups To: dpwillis67@gmail.com Friend, My colleagues and I are standing with doctors, veterans, over 100 humanitarian groups, and everyday people across the world who are demanding Israel’s government end the forced starvation of 2 million Palestinians in Gaza. Palestinian children are now dying daily from malnutrition, even though months’ worth of food is waiting right outside Gaza’s border. The UN has estimated that at least 14,000 infants could die if the blockade on Gaza continues. Two months ago, the U.S. government replaced Gaza’s humanitarian aid system with what the UN has called “death traps.” At these deadly “aid sites,” U.S. and Israeli forces have shot and killed more than 1,000 starving Palestinians trying to get food. We’re making noise in Congress to end this escalating genocide of my people. But most of my colleagues in Congress are still denying the genocide, while propping it up with their votes. The House of Representatives recently passed another massive military funding bill, with $600 million U.S. taxpayer dollars for Israel’s military.Four Democrats voted for an amendment to stop funding from going to the apartheid government of Israel: Summer Lee, Ilhan Omar, Al Green, and me. We know their courageous vote has made them a target by pro-genocide groups who want the killings and starvation to continue. body:
This is the moment they’ve warned us about.

| [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/112225667/559816353/1328794144?refcode=ea.c7c99cbc-fc6a-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&ms=ea.c7c99cbc-fc6a-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImM3Yzk5Y2JjLWZjNmEtZjAxMS04ZGM5LTYwNDViZGE5ZDk2YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=m9NTBrL4zjc49ltrBF0TR6iWvo20Xa-uUetMEt8eCG4=&emci=bc938ce0-7c69-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&emdi=c7c99cbc-fc6a-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&ceid=4823105)

At 87, I’ve lived through enough to know when a warning is real. For decades, we’ve been told we have time. That we can deal with the climate crisis in four years, after the next election, or when the right leader comes along. But that’s just not true anymore. **Our planet doesn’t have four more years. The fight is happening right here, right now.** **[That’s why I started JanePAC — to support climate champions who aren’t afraid to take on Big Oil and push for bold, urgent action. And right now, we’ve set a critical goal: $30,000 before the end of the month to help elect leaders who will meet this moment head-on. If you’re able, will you chip in today to help us get there?](https://click.everyaction.com/k/112225669/559816356/2105508926?refcode=ea.c7c99cbc-fc6a-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&ms=ea.c7c99cbc-fc6a-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImM3Yzk5Y2JjLWZjNmEtZjAxMS04ZGM5LTYwNDViZGE5ZDk2YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=m9NTBrL4zjc49ltrBF0TR6iWvo20Xa-uUetMEt8eCG4=)** The truth is, we are the first generation to truly feel the impacts of climate change, and the last that still has the power to stop it. Scientists have been clear. If we don’t dramatically cut greenhouse gas emissions soon, we are locking ourselves into a future of irreversible damage. But it doesn’t have to go that way. The billionaires and the fossil fuel corporations are fully involved in politics. And they count on us staying on the sides, unengaged. When we rise up and demonstrate people power, they’re scared of us. We have shown over and over again that the many can beat the money. **We can change course. We can protect what’s left. But it will take more than hope. It will take determination and people like you stepping up in moments like these when we need you most.** **[So I’m asking one more time because it’s _that_ important: Can I count on you to chip in by July 31 to help us hit our $30,000 goal and show that this movement is ready to meet the urgency of this moment?](https://click.everyaction.com/k/112225671/559816357/2105508926?refcode=ea.c7c99cbc-fc6a-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&ms=ea.c7c99cbc-fc6a-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImM3Yzk5Y2JjLWZjNmEtZjAxMS04ZGM5LTYwNDViZGE5ZDk2YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=m9NTBrL4zjc49ltrBF0TR6iWvo20Xa-uUetMEt8eCG4=)** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | [**DONATE $5**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/112225673/559816359/2105508926?express_lane=true&amount=5&refcode=ea.c7c99cbc-fc6a-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&ms=ea.c7c99cbc-fc6a-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImM3Yzk5Y2JjLWZjNmEtZjAxMS04ZGM5LTYwNDViZGE5ZDk2YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=m9NTBrL4zjc49ltrBF0TR6iWvo20Xa-uUetMEt8eCG4=) | [**DONATE $25**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/112225675/559816362/2105508926?express_lane=true&amount=25&refcode=ea.c7c99cbc-fc6a-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&ms=ea.c7c99cbc-fc6a-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImM3Yzk5Y2JjLWZjNmEtZjAxMS04ZGM5LTYwNDViZGE5ZDk2YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=m9NTBrL4zjc49ltrBF0TR6iWvo20Xa-uUetMEt8eCG4=)

clean_body: This is the moment they’ve warned us about. | At 87, I’ve lived through enough to know when a warning is real. For decades, we’ve been told we have time. That we can deal with the climate crisis in four years, after the next election, or when the right leader comes along. But that’s just not true anymore. Our planet doesn’t have four more years. The fight is happening right here, right now. = The truth is, we are the first generation to truly feel the impacts of climate change, and the last that still has the power to stop it. Scientists have been clear. If we don’t dramatically cut greenhouse gas emissions soon, we are locking ourselves into a future of irreversible damage. But it doesn’t have to go that way. The billionaires and the fossil fuel corporations are fully involved in politics. And they count on us staying on the sides, unengaged. When we rise up and demonstrate people power, they’re scared of us. We have shown over and over again that the many can beat the money. We can change course. We can protect what’s left. But it will take more than hope. It will take determination and people like you stepping up in moments like these when we need you most. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | = | = ---|--- = | = = | = We’re backing candidates who are brave enough to reject fossil fuel money, who are committed to climate justice, and who are ready to fight like hell for a livable future. name: Lucas Kunce
subject: Our mission
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Heartland Patriots (LucasKunce
body:
As we look forward to 2026 and beyond, we need to harness all of our grassroots energy into electing candidates who will stand strong for working families.
#
Peter — when I decided to run for office, I didn’t have a list of wealthy donors or a famous last name to help me do it. I had supporters like you.
Together, we built a record-breaking grassroots movement fueled by donations from real working people. We didn’t take any money from shady mega-donors or corporate PACs. Nothing from Big Oil or Big Pharma executives.
I’m still proud of everything we accomplished together and I’ll always be grateful for your unwavering support. **But now, as we look forward to 2026 and beyond, we need to harness all of our energy into electing candidates who are committed to standing strong for working families and protecting all of our freedoms.**
**That’s why my team and I launched Heartland Patriots — to make sure these candidates have the resources to compete against the influence of Big Money and WIN.[If you’re ready to fuel our mission, will you please chip in $10 or whatever you can today? We’re building an entirely grassroots movement, powered by people like you, so anything you can give really will make a big impact.](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/92730?refcode=em20250727-9177&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=9177%2E614464%2E50uMuY)**

clean_body: As we look forward to 2026 and beyond, we need to harness all of our grassroots energy into electing candidates who will stand strong for working families. Peter — when I decided to run for office, I didn’t have a list of wealthy donors or a famous last name to help me do it. I had supporters like you. Together, we built a record-breaking grassroots movement fueled by donations from real working people. We didn’t take any money from shady mega-donors or corporate PACs. Nothing from Big Oil or Big Pharma executives. I’m still proud of everything we accomplished together and I’ll always be grateful for your unwavering support. But now, as we look forward to 2026 and beyond, we need to harness all of our energy into electing candidates who are committed to standing strong for working families and protecting all of our freedoms. That’s why my team and I launched Heartland Patriots — to make sure these candidates have the resources to compete against the influence of Big Money and WIN. name: Team Haaland HQ
subject: 121,312 square miles
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY DEB FOR NEW MEXICO
body:
Traveling across the entire state is essential to scaling up our campaign.
# [](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/133?t=1&akid=3431%2E400544%2EfZNKT-)
Hey,
Did you know that **New Mexico is the FIFTH-largest state** in the country by land area?! 🌄
121,312 square miles of beautiful mountains, farmlands, cities and towns of all sizes – **and Deb Haaland is going to cross all of it.**
Here’s the only issue: Because New Mexico is such a big state, even something as necessary as traveling to meet voters isn’t just timely, but also very expensive. Our campaign’s ability to host town halls, organize meet-and-greets, and even fill up the gas tank will all depend on how much money we raise.
**[Could you please chip in $5 to keep Deb on the road? Hitting our $60,000 fundraising goal before this Thursday will mean Deb can keep meeting voters all across our (very large) state.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20250727_1_5largeststate?amount=5&refcode=em20250727-3431&refcode2=3431_400544_fZNKT-&akid=3431%2E400544%2EfZNKT-)**
**[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20250727_1_5largeststate?amount=5&refcode=em20250727-3431&refcode2=3431_400544_fZNKT-&akid=3431%2E400544%2EfZNKT-)**
Every campaign-hosted event presents another major opportunity for Deb to meet hundreds of new people. Every single person she speaks to is a potential new supporter for our campaign. That is why traveling across _the entire state_ is essential to scaling up our people-powered movement for New Mexico as quickly as possible.
**[Deb is determined to cross all of New Mexico to meet new voters. But without hitting our goal, that simply can’t happen. Will you donate $5 before Thursday’s deadline to keep our campaign on the road?](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20250727_1_5largeststate?amount=5&refcode=em20250727-3431&refcode2=3431_400544_fZNKT-&akid=3431%2E400544%2EfZNKT-)**
Thanks for making this all possible. See you out there! 🚗
Team Haaland HQ
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_footer?refcode=em20250727-1&t=2&refcode2=3431_400544_fZNKT-&akid=3431%2E400544%2EfZNKT-)**

clean_body: | = The Democrats' fight to retake one of California’s most flippable House districts just hit a breaking point. Republican David Valadao -- the incumbent in California’s 22nd District -- shocked even members of his own party by voting for Trump’s so-called “Better Budget Balance” Act, which slashes Medicaid by nearly $1 trillion. = Nearly 70% of Valadao’s district relies on Medicaid today. Despite repeated promises to his voters that he’d never vote for cuts to the program, he backed it anyway. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | = | = | | = | = | | = | = Voters are furious, and this race has emerged as ground zero in the fight to defend health care access and defeat the GOP’s extremist, corporate-first agenda. The good news is that Valadao’s district is one of the most flippable in the country. It’s swung for Democrats in the past, and 2026 represents the best shot we’ve ever had to oust Valadao for good. Democrat Randy Villegas, a 30-year-old college professor and son of immigrants, is running to unseat Valadao -- and he’s not taking a dime from corporate PACs. Randy has deep roots in the Central Valley, a strong local base of support, and a real shot at flipping this seat. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | = | = | | = | = | | = | = \- California Election Update | With deep ties to our community and a lifelong commitment to justice and equity, Randy is running for Congress in California’s 22nd District to bring bold, compassionate leadership to Washington. name: Squad Victory Fund
subject: Public media institutions are under attack
body:
[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/18910?refcode=em20250727-1450-foote&t=1001&akid=1450%2E342411%2E4XaWHg ]Squad Victory Fund

Peter,

Congress recently approved a devastating $1.1 billion federal funding cut
to public media institutions like PBS and NPR.^1

This isn’t just about national programming. These media outlets are
lifelines in communities across the country. They tell local stories, hold
the powerful accountable, and provide critical information during
wildfires, floods, and other emergencies. In many rural areas, they are
the only reliable source of local news.^2

Now, that infrastructure is under threat. These cuts jeopardize the
futures of dozens of stations — especially small and rural ones that
depend on federal funding.

If these stations go dark, so do the voices of the communities they serve.
And that’s no accident — this is a calculated attack on truth,
transparency, and connection.

That’s why the Squad is stepping up to fight back: To defend public media
and turn up the pressure to restore funding. But to do that, we need your
help.

[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/19213?c0=svfem-20250727-movement.W_quiq&refcode=em20250727-1450&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1002&akid=1450%2E342411%2E4XaWHg ]Will you chip in $3 to the Squad Victory Fund ahead of
our end-of-month deadline to fight back against these dangerous cuts,
defend trusted journalism, and protect local voices?

[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/19213?c0=svfem-20250727-movement.W_quiq&refcode=em20250727-1450&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1021&akid=1450%2E342411%2E4XaWHg ]Contribute ››

We can’t afford to let public media wither while corporate interests grow
louder. Help us stand up, speak out, and fight back.

Thank you,

The Squad Victory Fund

1 — [ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/19935?t=1022&akid=1450%2E342411%2E4XaWHg ]Politico

In addition to what’s legally required, The Squad Victory Fund does not accept contributions from: (1) corporations, associations, or their PACs; (2) individuals registered as federal lobbyists or under FARA; (3) from the PACs, lobbyists, or SEC-named executives of fossil fuel companies; or (4) from the Fraternal Order of Police.

Contributions or gifts to The Squad Victory Fund are not tax deductible.

Contributions to The Squad Victory Fund will be allocated as follows: 8.33% to each of Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, Rashida Tlaib for Congress, Courage to Change, Inspiring Leadership Has a Name PAC, Power of Us PAC, Rooted in Community Leadership PAC, Politivist PAC, and For the Children PAC.

Any contributor may designate their contribution for a particular participant. The allocation formula above may change if any contributor makes a contribution that, when allocated, would exceed the amount that the contributor may lawfully give to any participant.

After contributing to The Squad Victory Fund, you will automatically be signed up to receive email updates from Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, and Rashida Tlaib for Congress. You may of course opt-out by unsubscribing at any time.

To contribute via check, please address to The Squad Victory Fund c/o MBA Consulting Group, PO Box 15845, Washington D.C., 20003

clean_body: [ ]Squad Victory Fund Peter, Congress recently approved a devastating $1.1 billion federal funding cut to public media institutions like PBS and NPR.^1 This isn’t just about national programming. These media outlets are lifelines in communities across the country. They tell local stories, hold the powerful accountable, and provide critical information during wildfires, floods, and other emergencies. In many rural areas, they are the only reliable source of local news.^2 Now, that infrastructure is under threat. These cuts jeopardize the futures of dozens of stations — especially small and rural ones that depend on federal funding. If these stations go dark, so do the voices of the communities they serve. And that’s no accident — this is a calculated attack on truth, transparency, and connection. That’s why the Squad is stepping up to fight back: To defend public media and turn up the pressure to restore funding. But to do that, we need your help. [ ]Will you chip in $3 to the Squad Victory Fund ahead of our end-of-month deadline to fight back against these dangerous cuts, defend trusted journalism, and protect local voices? [ ]Contribute ›› We can’t afford to let public media wither while corporate interests grow louder. Help us stand up, speak out, and fight back. Thank you, The Squad Victory Fund 1 — [ ]Politico 2 — [ ]Houston Chronicle

We’ve set aside some time in Katie’s calendar this week for her to make calls to grassroots supporters, and we wanted to make sure you didn’t miss out on this chance to get a call from Katie!
**[When you donate any amount through this email, you’ll automatically be added onto the list of phone numbers that Katie might call.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_kp_20250727_1_ak?refcode=em20250727-39924&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&refcode2=39924_3422442_2unSNC&akid=39924%2E3422442%2E2unSNC)**
This campaign is about empowering everyday people to make their voices heard. Katie doesn’t want to hear from the same few voices; she wants to hear the best ideas from everyone, no matter where they come from, and turn them into policy to make Californians’ lives better.
Here on Team Porter, we believe that whether you give the maximum amount to our campaign or you just chip in whatever you can spare, you have just as much of a stake in our democracy as anyone else.
Your voice matters.
**[So please, consider giving $5 or whatever you can today, and you just might be receiving a call from Katie in the very near future!](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_kp_20250727_1_ak?refcode=em20250727-39924&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&refcode2=39924_3422442_2unSNC&akid=39924%2E3422442%2E2unSNC)**

Onward and upward,
Team KP

Katie is running to be California’s next governor because California and our country need a leader who will stand up to Trump to lower costs, protect our rights, and combat climate change. **[Donate ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4g-em-footer?refcode=em20250727-39924&amounts=&t=3&refcode2=39924_3422442_2unSNC&akid=39924%2E3422442%2E2unSNC)**

name: Qasim Rashid, Esq.
subject: Why 300+ Paid Subscribers Have Cancelled
body:
And why I am doubling down on my human rights advocacy anyway

| |
---|---|---
| | | Forwarded this email? [Subscribe here](https://substack..FgjKIOvPxzdc8Z1RKs4cH1QR5NloefsLbRQ-46h9NTM?) for more
---
[](https://substack..zogYpQempnxSyy8Nk3I-xvoAPHg66aLy0Wy3c3yyGbI?)
---
Thank you for being an awesome subscriber! When possible, I hope you'll consider upgrading to a $6/mo or $50/year subscription!
* * *
# [Why 300+ Paid Subscribers Have Cancelled](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2550223&post_id=169380077&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY5MzgwMDc3LCJpYXQiOjE3NTM2MzI3MjksImV4cCI6MTc1NjIyNDcyOSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI1NTAyMjMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.UGKnHjiAhs_bIATYp7yp2f2HgPfLvEYHG9jRKQdlVrI)
### And why I am doubling down on my human rights advocacy anyway
| | [Qasim Rashid, Esq.](https://substack.com/@qasimrashid)
---
| Jul 27
---
| [](https://substack.com/@qasimrashid)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2550223&post_id=169380077&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY5MzgwMDc3LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc1MzYzMjcyOSwiZXhwIjoxNzU2MjI0NzI5LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMjU1MDIyMyIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.kcBnBXgaFJxwnto48b1y2rwORPbO7eMDIDLcSpSarpw&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2550223&post_id=169380077&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY5MzgwMDc3LCJpYXQiOjE3NTM2MzI3MjksImV4cCI6MTc1NjIyNDcyOSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI1NTAyMjMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.UGKnHjiAhs_bIATYp7yp2f2HgPfLvEYHG9jRKQdlVrI&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2550223&post_id=169380077&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY5MzgwMDc3LCJpYXQiOjE3NTM2MzI3MjksImV4cCI6MTc1NjIyNDcyOSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI1NTAyMjMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.UGKnHjiAhs_bIATYp7yp2f2HgPfLvEYHG9jRKQdlVrI)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9vcGVuLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9wdWIvcWFzaW1yYXNoaWQvcC93aHktMzAwLXBhaWQtc3Vic2NyaWJlcnMtaGF2ZS1jYW5jZWxsZWQ_.VQASUQ2QgBPRj8_af5b39q7Vi7Jdl0Ikt-TiErP0xqw?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/qasimrashid/p/why-300-paid-subscribers-have-cancelled?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

Dear Friends -
I need to write a brutally honest piece. Here's the thing -- I've been resolute since Day 1 to condemn Israel's genocide on Palestinians, [with detailed updates along the way citing every receipt and aspect of human rights law](https://substack.com/redirect/d8694d0c-ad27-41ca-9dfc-a5555db9cb9c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). I've raised my voice at every opportunity, in fact, during my last 15 years working in human rights law--and even before then. And I've especially elevated my platform these last three months as the assault on Gaza has reached beyond a breaking point. **Let 's Address This.**
**Let's Address This with Qasim Rashid is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber.**
[ Upgrade to paid](https://substack..akOwtTiFvkizopcoFlaVHyLtQpz0oitJFtAgULwwZfE?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=169380077)
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/f27e6f53-81c8-41a4-8fbe-6446a6534d62?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Honesty requires me to show vulnerability, because after all my human rights advocacy is also my livelihood. Thus, today I can report that over these last 3 months, more than 300 paid subscribers have cancelled their subscriptions to my human rights newsletter, Let's Address This with Qasim Rashid. Yes, 300+ Paid Subscribers, with a significant number of people citing my advocacy for Gaza as the reason.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/db7ebf7e-e437-4f68-ad41-9cfbdf6a5c74?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
When subscribers cancel, Substack asks them to share their reason for cancellation. Here is a screenshot of what former subscribers have shared.
Now, I have two options. One, I can either tone down my advocacy, maybe even eliminate it altogether. Perhaps that would better protect my livelihood.
Or two, I can do what's right and unapologetically double down on my advocacy for universal human rights because it is the right thing to do, period.

So let me be absolutely clear to those who have cancelled their paid subscriptions due to my advocacy for universal human rights: Unsubscribe. Block. Cancel. Report. Call me names. Do whatever the hell it is you need to do.
**I will not stop condemning the genocide committed by the Israeli government against the Palestinian people.**

This is not about money. This is about humanity. Our collective humanity.
I am a human rights lawyer. I am a human being. And I will not be silent in the face of injustice, no matter the cost. **I** **am proud** to stand on the right side of history with millions of people--Muslims, Christians, Jews, Sikhs, people of all faiths and no faith--for the sake of justice. Just as I unapologetically spoke up for the children of Israel on 10/7, I unapologetically speak up for the children of Palestine, just as I would speak up for my own children. Why? Because as the great James Baldwin once said, "_The children are always ours, every single one of them, all over the globe; and I am beginning to suspect that whoever is incapable of recognizing this may be incapable of morality_." Likewise, the Torah and Qur'an both embrace this beautiful teaching that "_To kill one person is to kill all humanity, but to save one life is to save all humanity_."
Children are starving to death. Our basic morality is on the line. And we cannot fail this moral test. We cannot afford the price of silence.
Thus, I seek support only from people committed to universal human rights, never from those who think money buys my silence on genocide. So, to the naysayers I say unapologetically--do your worst. It will not faze me, it will not slow me down, and it absolutely will not stop me from speaking up for justice for Palestine.
And lastly, to the politicians issuing empty statements that "starvation is bad, we should do something," yet refusing to even acknowledge that maybe, **just** **maybe** , they should stop funding the war criminal Netanyahu enforcing the starvation--please know that your empty statements are useless and you remain complicit in this genocide. "Never again" demands action, otherwise its an empty and meaningless slogan. Either **do something** , or resign and let someone with actual courage lead.

Stop the genocide. Stop the war crimes. Stop the starvation. FREE PALESTINE.
Sincerely,
Qasim

**P.S.** To those who have remained as paid subscribers, please know I have immense gratitude to you for your trust, sincerity, and generosity. From the bottom of my heart, thank you for standing up with courage and solidarity in this critical moment. ✊🏽
And finally, for those seeking to support my human rights advocacy--with the knowledge that I will always unapologetically speak up for the human dignity of every person on Earth--I earnestly welcome your support. And I invite you to subscribe for free or for a cup of coffee a month at the link below. Much love to you all. ❤️
**Let's Address This with Qasim Rashid is a human rights newsletter that unapologetically demands universal human rights. Join our movement by subscribing.**
[ Upgrade to paid](https://substack..akOwtTiFvkizopcoFlaVHyLtQpz0oitJFtAgULwwZfE?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=169380077)
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/1e9c8c38-62e3-4fcc-8460-f677b566521c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
**Quick Ask!** I am doing everything in my power over the next four years to stave off fascism in the United States, and I need your support.**Become a paid subscriber and join the movement.**
[ Upgrade to paid](https://substack..akOwtTiFvkizopcoFlaVHyLtQpz0oitJFtAgULwwZfE?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=169380077)

clean_body: But now that you’re a Cabinet-Level Advisor, I need you to answer a few questions. | | | | | Friend, I made a big decision at the White House! In consultation with Vice President JD Vance, I made the call to open up a few more highly selective spots for Cabinet-Level Advisors... AND THE FIRST ONE IS GOING TO YOU! We can celebrate your promotion tomorrow. Right now we need to get to work! I’ve personally put together this survey for any new Cabinet-Level Advisors to take on day one of their new position, so I’m going to need you to complete it ASAP. I’ll check back for your responses tomorrow. Thank you for everything that you do, Donald J. subject: This is historic
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Ted Cruz for Senate
body:
We just made history, and now is not the time to let up | [](http://links.makingamericathegreatest.com/u/click?_t=79fa4a3daf024a0a886c5b498ff32e32&_m=d641008ecbe64b72a5ec91c53253355f&_e=f0XPnpTGakCF-4_OcRtVqCmYeHf2c5IZ5caEvZXMUhxiNkdWHcvpzdITu5X7xrLREZfLpRCYvY4qYuhXEjM6Xy9FXrB7QMZ1o1MM6gotRfWiHwClywI674GLwthq0ZdXABolj8oWw2MpVAqfLH8AXoTNrEu4wu-uhmAmCgv8eRm4QtrirxQh7ceyhPMw9zCCtFO-Ldmb_jqSN9Oy6FhP-oIyhcA_BSpPBYNertPd_DSldZK-ObRX5gGjU90Dw0AMCX02s7oEKxZCsoOsO7ks-Mw2P79xri6Px0HCl4l5I8rdFrsCtrFXotSU0TJwsj1Tjq_hvGH2Y1vLZbbNUeEl_POs48N9zs8nDzXj-5euA5t9GLIzlrpPFSI7o1v9Zx-2vsDrLvSefpJf2lwlJNA8JUVbbKLoFLq0haRomiiQw-CJWUVTSdae-Ty1jyRHqU_oRmwc-utVGKpgNG9NZOHXaSNaHx1zQJ5rpT8cgDEsc8i2bqNFKOCdgKaPghccHtNy8XNg01spFmDCnalxK-F2WFFrovdEy777XC7QDoQ_zISJxxx_qIp30_OJqgDa_cuJAz4r4iW3qcdoUEQbzJW95nCKvhqkc4xmX9bBKRJZptl-vuq8eVCa2XwpwvDBr6QYYRokBbftys3s6eqEP6bRDSQxpNZ7Avvul8NM10ynzZyJbadelzsLr5KdIvFTKMI_x5FgnWlSx3IWtWlPmddZ0p5tFWq0iC6ujC8NGDenUDBtK819EAEsp6R-p2YvqOmEZTUauckxrYgi6k2kdlNHnGUjuHOsNGxw00sGbnn-iiOYhsAUMBwKtt7KMMpMFGzBiT20IiqDnxLJ47Uz0KDLuJIyaCALxP-yxuECh9DFqk_73r3IHYLFxYnDuaXfZDrURnX5cHQIfhnKuA6F-A4FCpzQLa0fZwCJHusCWJ1us5Y%3D)

---

Fellow Conservative,

**I need to ask you something that might feel small — but matters a lot.**

**Will you[chip in just $1](http://links.makingamericathegreatest.com/u/click?_t=79fa4a3daf024a0a886c5b498ff32e32&_m=d641008ecbe64b72a5ec91c53253355f&_e=f0XPnpTGakCF-4_OcRtVqCmYeHf2c5IZ5caEvZXMUhxiNkdWHcvpzdITu5X7xrLREZfLpRCYvY4qYuhXEjM6Xy9FXrB7QMZ1o1MM6gotRfWiHwClywI674GLwthq0ZdXABolj8oWw2MpVAqfLH8AXoTNrEu4wu-uhmAmCgv8eRm4QtrirxQh7ceyhPMw9zCCtFO-Ldmb_jqSN9Oy6FhP-oIyhcA_BSpPBYNertPd_DSldZK-ObRX5gGjU90Dw0AMCX02s7oEKxZCsoOsO7ks-Mw2P79xri6Px0HCl4l5I8rdFrsCtrFXotSU0TJwsj1Tjq_hvGH2Y1vLZbbNUeEl_POs48N9zs8nDzXj-5euA5vMSaQU0XELeLsWVuP6LpBooe1_8tNhNYyCeWf6zZGnDl6r8zUZBPIk4tUd9JUOFK40qllH-JuC2oSkN2WptM16Osp-Ga5cDm73yCAlaAuOs9BteewWgl_g8cQ4G6Sg18KVpcphOknu01yLwgSLMGrTCp_QUQc8IvNTjpNSRE90M3-tgW6fMx1Z5CMl9PvXH1q7iW_qKC612GMIzwiHklwO88MyXSoHRQeKQZZAFKfN-0Q4RnKC3c446iJewliceSQ9bGGsyVAB8FAjsJGtFNaf-xIF905_3W63pcdn4eaucqgoTg2g-Se72X5Fc6fIw5lsuQRXOY0_dVA-uXn9k54wfjidk-sMEq255WPXIhS6U8c35zCQ9AjpCcEp_ZZLSRPQsMaZr3lMAHIW0eS1SphoSjLF5MQ6DaQq5VLGV8kqk5edz2QMuGKLan_Oum1YVWvpuLDwBexstnjiGVwKwIn09zzQBuv6SDtzQ-xGlC7mEfXkGZTggmpHdv3t9Blf_F2fMYK2y5ntdyTQc88oF7PpljZRfuBHtj5PDo3tUeepD7E0Ihyi6vK6Ya29XUwuQt8%3D) today to help us stop the Democrats from taking the Senate?
**
You may not have donated in a while. I get it. name: Jeff Merkley
subject: Renters deserve transparency
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jeff Merkley for Oregon
body:
Too many corporate landlords are using surprise fees, inflated penalties, and expensive credit checks to squeeze more money out of renters.
| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/112230551/559831759/150430950?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDEvMS84OTMyNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3ZWNjYTIxMS0wNzZiLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=A_jxkv5T0hDBEzkWCwuEp8PE7BLE3AhPQOfXEzBXYBI=&emci=588e3ba4-8069-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&emdi=7ecca211-076b-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&ceid=7731215)

| | If you've ever rented a home or apartment, you've probably had this experience: You finally find a new home in your budget and get ready to sign the lease — only to get slammed with hundreds of dollars of "junk fees" for things like application or credit screening fees. That's why I've introduced the End Junk Fees for Renters Act with Representative Maxwell Frost to crack down on corporate landlords and bring transparency and accountability to the rental market. Too many corporate landlords are using bogus surprise fees, inflated penalties, and expensive credit checks to squeeze more money out of renters. It's legalized exploitation, plain and simple, and it's making housing even more unaffordable for families across the country. Our bill is simple, commonsense legislation:
* No more hidden or surprise rental fees
* Upfront, clear pricing for all charges
* Fair access to housing — not arbitrary credit barriers

Let's get rid of these junk fees because all Americans deserve a fair shot at a safe, affordable roof over their heads and the power to fight back against these predatory charges. [**Everyone deserves a fair shot at safe, affordable housing without being nickel-and-dimed at every turn. Will you stand with us and demand transparency for renters?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/112230552/559831760/1872157636?contactdata=BpkyCkeOmcPOa7alACInKrLOJWuE11sEuSyxBuaUSbPzLnIF5k1MYVO4JFoqxxNjAnoCxaub4z1fJeNXus3zydkhuQ+jXtDwRjpekhHsAS41MPplr+woi3doFtWMOXZMHFg+W/xMSi5dDdlYI/PGBw==&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDEvMS84OTMyNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3ZWNjYTIxMS0wNzZiLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=A_jxkv5T0hDBEzkWCwuEp8PE7BLE3AhPQOfXEzBXYBI=&emci=588e3ba4-8069-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&emdi=7ecca211-076b-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&ceid=7731215) [Sign the Petition](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/112230553/559831761/1872157636?contactdata=BpkyCkeOmcPOa7alACInKrLOJWuE11sEuSyxBuaUSbPzLnIF5k1MYVO4JFoqxxNjAnoCxaub4z1fJeNXus3zydkhuQ+jXtDwRjpekhHsAS41MPplr+woi3doFtWMOXZMHFg+W/xMSi5dDdlYI/PGBw==&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDEvMS84OTMyNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3ZWNjYTIxMS0wNzZiLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=A_jxkv5T0hDBEzkWCwuEp8PE7BLE3AhPQOfXEzBXYBI=&emci=588e3ba4-8069-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&emdi=7ecca211-076b-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&ceid=7731215) Onward! _Jeff_ | Express Donate: If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution to Jeff Merkley instantly:

clean_body: Too many corporate landlords are using surprise fees, inflated penalties, and expensive credit checks to squeeze more money out of renters. | | | | If you've ever rented a home or apartment, you've probably had this experience: You finally find a new home in your budget and get ready to sign the lease — only to get slammed with hundreds of dollars of "junk fees" for things like application or credit screening fees. That's why I've introduced the End Junk Fees for Renters Act with Representative Maxwell Frost to crack down on corporate landlords and bring transparency and accountability to the rental market. Too many corporate landlords are using bogus surprise fees, inflated penalties, and expensive credit checks to squeeze more money out of renters. It's legalized exploitation, plain and simple, and it's making housing even more unaffordable for families across the country. Our bill is simple, commonsense legislation: No more hidden or surprise rental fees Upfront, clear pricing for all charges Fair access to housing — not arbitrary credit barriers Let's get rid of these junk fees because all Americans deserve a fair shot at a safe, affordable roof over their heads and the power to fight back against these predatory charges. Onward! name: Steel River GoPack
subject: When the grid goes down, what will you do.
body:
You don’t need a bunker. You need this...
| | | [](https://links.gunowner-news.com/a/1537/click/21682/6374453/c57e37f44275a071ddc18bee52ed591ed372d2ff/4dac8af7d7117264ed1b2a30502b98951f97b299)
---
| | |
---
_July 27th, 2025 at 08:55 PM UTC_
---
| | |
---
| | Hey, Not trying to be dramatic, but if the last few years have taught us anything, it’s this: **You can’t count on the system to bail you out.** That’s why we’re giving away 200 of our **Steel River Go Packs**. It’s a simple survival kit we built for regular people who just want to be a little more prepared. It’s got:
* Waterproof matches
* A tough little multitool
* Solar charger
* Headlamp
* Emergency baton — flashlight, area light, and flashing red SOS mode with a magnetic base
* And a spork that’s actually kind of badass

No fluff. Just useful gear. Just pay shipping and it's yours. If you've been meaning to get your act together — even just a little — this is an easy first step. [Get your free Go Pack here while they’re still available](https://links.gunowner-news.com/a/1537/click/21682/6374453/b084e3d434dbcc7ca5f35906e998b9f30fa895bb/4dac8af7d7117264ed1b2a30502b98951f97b299) Stay sharp,
– Mike
Steel River Team P.S. These usually go fast when we open up a new batch. If the link doesn’t work, we’re out.

name: Sara Jacobs
subject: re: my visit to Otay Mesa Detention Center
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Sara Jacobs for Congress
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sjj-em [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sjj-em]
Hi friends,
Last week I visited the Otay Mesa Detention Center to do oversight of how the Department of Homeland Security is housing immigration detainees there. Here’s what I saw:
At the facility, I spoke with folks from ICE and representatives from CoreCivic – the private company that runs the center – and I asked a lot of questions.
Importantly, I was able to bring up medical, legal, and language access issues and push for answers because of everyone in the community who reached out and shared what they’ve seen and heard. I’m grateful to all the folks who’ve been calling and emailing my office – thank you!
Overall, the transparency level that they gave us was sufficient – but let’s be real, we all know these facilities prepare for visits like mine. They clean up. They rehearse. And they think about what we want to hear. That’s why all of you sharing your stories is so important – so I can see past the PR and spin and know what’s really going on.
I’m going to continue showing up, and continue asking hard questions – not just because it’s my job (which it is – conducting oversight is a responsibility of Members of Congress), but because our community deserves transparency, accountability, and dignity. ICE promised answers to my questions and concerns, and I’m going to hold them to it.
Our community deserves to know what is being done in our name. Because I don’t believe that what the Trump Administration is doing on immigration is making us safer – in fact, it’s making us less safe. Most of the people held at Otay Mesa are there because of civil violations and not violent crimes – meanwhile, they are detained by ICE agents in riot gear while at work or arrested when they’re showing up as asked at their immigration court hearings.
So while I keep pushing back, I ask that you keep it up, too. Keep showing up for your neighbors and speaking out against injustices – that’s how we actually make a difference right now.
Thanks for reading.
-Sara
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sjj-em [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sjj-em]
Congresswoman Sara Jacobs
California’s 51st District

subject: This is my final email, Peter
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Winsome for Governor
clean_body: - This is my final message to you before time expires. Knowing that, will you please read every word carefully? Thanks for opening this email! I wouldn't be reaching out like this if my situation weren't truly dire. Let me explain my predicament: FIRST, while I was barred from accepting any campaign donations during Virginia's legislative session, my Democrat opponent, Abigail Spanberger, was RAKING IN hundreds of thousands of dollars! THEN, a Soros-backed, liberal group gave my Democrat opponent $500K all in one shot! My Democrat opponent has continued to OUTRAISE me by MILLIONS of dollars. NOW, the polls are TIED. But I cannot win with this crippling cash disadvantage. TO MAKE MATTERS WORSE, I'm facing a CRUCIAL Mid-Month Deadline Goal and I'm WAY behind! in a serious bind here, Peter. I'm over $10,000 behind pace to reach my upcoming Goal. I'm asking you as a Christian, a Marine, and a mother: Democrat opponent has Leftist billionaires like George Soros and Hollywood liberals backing her, but I'm 100% relying on you! can't thank you enough for reading this message all the way through, Peter. You are truly a key part of my team! Together, we will close this fundraising gap and beat my liberal opponent in November! Thanks again and may God bless you! -Winsome Earle-Sears | | | | | | Paid for by Winsome for Governor Military disclaimer: Neither military info nor photographs of Winsome Earle-Sears in uniform imply endorsement of Winsome Earle-Sears by the Department of Defense or its particular military departments. body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-threespecials?refcode=072725_1 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-threespecials?refcode=072725_1]
This is one of the most brazen power grabs we’ve seen.
Texans are still dealing with the aftermath of catastrophic flooding — and Greg Abbott is already exploiting the crisis to redraw congressional maps and tip the scales for Trump in 2026.
This is happening in real time. And if we don’t act now, it won’t stop with Texas. Rush a donation now to help stop this scheme before it’s too late >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-abbott?refcode=072725_1]
Greg Abbott is hijacking a disaster — and we need your help to stop him.
Before we explain how bad this is, here’s what we need from you:
👉 Chip in now to help stop Abbott’s blatant power grab and defend democracy in Texas >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-abbott?refcode=072725_1]
Texans are grieving. Families are still searching for the missing.
Floodwaters haven’t even receded — and Abbott just called a special session of the legislature.
Not for relief.
Not for recovery.
But to redraw congressional maps mid-cycle and give Trump a partisan edge in 2026.
This isn’t disaster response — it’s a calculated, authoritarian move to rig the next election while the public is focused on survival.
Texas Democrats are already mobilizing, but they can’t do this alone. They need legal firepower, organizing funds, and rapid-response communications to fight this from every angle.
This is the blueprint for MAGA power grabs nationwide — and if Abbott wins here, others will follow. Rush a donation now to help stop this scheme before it’s too late >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-abbott?refcode=072725_1]

name: Wes Moore
subject: Military school was off to a rocky start
body:
[ https://act.wesmoore.com/go/9?t=1001&akid=366%2E1086043%2EZo7Giq ]Wes Moore for Governor[ https://act.wesmoore.com/go/9?t=1002&akid=366%2E1086043%2EZo7Giq ]Wes Moore for Governor

Everyone has specific moments that are pivotal in their
lives, Peter.

Before I tell you my story, [ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wes-em-20250727-1?refcode=em-20250727-1&refcode2=366_1086043_Zo7Giq&akid=366%2E1086043%2EZo7Giq ]will you pitch into my campaign today?
We’re coming up on our end-of-month deadline quickly.

My first few months of military school, I was off to a rocky start.

I was homesick. I was under strict discipline. To say I was unhappy was an
understatement.

I saw no positives to the situation, so I tried to escape. I ran away —
regularly.

In fact, I ran away so many times, my sergeant leader drew me a “map” of
how to get to the train station so I could finally go home.

That night, I learned I could not read a map. I was lost in the woods,
crying, and would take any help I could get.

What happened after that?

My chain of command found me and took me back to school.

They gave me five minutes to call my mom. I begged her to come take me
home, but she stood her ground. She said she sacrificed too much for me to
leave, so I stayed.

In all honesty, I think I grew up in that moment. I wasn’t going anywhere
— literally or figuratively — so I decided to seek out what possibilities
were ahead.

I started to take myself seriously. I learned how to look out for others.
And what was initially a tough time at the very beginning of my military
career turned into a needed opportunity to serve myself and others.

Now, as Maryland's
governor, I have made it my mission to leave no one behind. That is why we
are listening to and serving everyone. Without the countless voices
pitching in, we would not be able to make this Maryland’s decade.

But I need your help so I can continue to serve, and continue fighting for
everyone across our state.

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wes-em-20250727-1?refcode=em-20250727-1&refcode2=366_1086043_Zo7Giq&akid=366%2E1086043%2EZo7Giq ]We’re coming up on a major end-of-month deadline and need to hit our
goals. Will you pitch in any amount today?

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wes-em-20250727-1?amount=5&refcode=em-20250727-1&refcode2=366_1086043_Zo7Giq&akid=366%2E1086043%2EZo7Giq ]Donate $5 ››

I’m no longer running away — in fact, I’m running towards the hard stuff.

That includes helping
Marylanders wherever I
can.

Peter, I
appreciate you running toward the fight, too.

Elevate,

Wes

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wes-em-20250727-1?refcode=em-20250727-1&refcode2=366_1086043_Zo7Giq&akid=366%2E1086043%2EZo7Giq ]Wes Moore

You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time:
http://act.wesmoore.com/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=366%2E1086043%2EZo7Giq

name: Emily Randall
subject: Emily’s Notes: Bringing it home
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Emily Randall for Congress
body:
Welcome to the latest edition of Emily’s Notes — updates from Washington’s 6th and Washington, D.C.!

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/112233200/559841522/2145945666?refcode=RS_EM_EN_2025.07.25_JulyNewsletter_Actives&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwMDJkYWY5MS0xMDZiLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=LMYInMcmtIUewl8pR64M6a_Y0AKe-jtUK_A4vmJCUqw=&emci=f4a4d933-d568-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&emdi=002daf91-106b-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&ceid=23483442) | Peter – Welcome to the latest edition of Emily’s Notes — updates from Washington’s 6th and Washington, D.C.! Here’s what I’ve been up to: • **First, some positive news** – We’re bringing _$18 __million _home to Washington’s 6th District for infrastructure improvements, expanded access to health care, and public safety initiatives! These funded projects cover all six counties in the 6th District – from building a shelter for severe weather events in Clallam County to updating outdated health care records systems in Grays Harbor County. [_**See the full list here →**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/112233202/559841524/-1266616535?refcode=RS_EM_EN_2025.07.25_JulyNewsletter_Actives&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwMDJkYWY5MS0xMDZiLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=LMYInMcmtIUewl8pR64M6a_Y0AKe-jtUK_A4vmJCUqw=&emci=f4a4d933-d568-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&emdi=002daf91-106b-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&ceid=23483442) • **Cleanup at Indian Creek** – Governor Ferguson and I visited the site where a fuel tanker overturned at Indian Creek, threatening the Elwha River and drinking water for the city of Port Angeles. We’ll work with state, local, and Tribal leaders to clean our river and protect our drinking water. [_**Watch my video update →**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/112233203/559841525/-218595979?hl=en&refcode=RS_EM_EN_2025.07.25_JulyNewsletter_Actives&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwMDJkYWY5MS0xMDZiLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=LMYInMcmtIUewl8pR64M6a_Y0AKe-jtUK_A4vmJCUqw=&emci=f4a4d933-d568-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&emdi=002daf91-106b-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&ceid=23483442) [_****_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/112233205/559841527/1097742672?hl=en&refcode=RS_EM_EN_2025.07.25_JulyNewsletter_Actives&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwMDJkYWY5MS0xMDZiLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=LMYInMcmtIUewl8pR64M6a_Y0AKe-jtUK_A4vmJCUqw=&emci=f4a4d933-d568-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&emdi=002daf91-106b-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&ceid=23483442) • **Providing an ANCHOR** – I grew up around many military families and know the difficulties faced during relocation. I introduced bipartisan legislation to address those issues: the Assuring Navigation of Compact Help For Ongoing Relocation (ANCHOR) for Military Families Act. [_**Learn more →**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/112233207/559841530/977512431?refcode=RS_EM_EN_2025.07.25_JulyNewsletter_Actives&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwMDJkYWY5MS0xMDZiLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=LMYInMcmtIUewl8pR64M6a_Y0AKe-jtUK_A4vmJCUqw=&emci=f4a4d933-d568-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&emdi=002daf91-106b-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&ceid=23483442) • **You deserve a raise** – I’ll be chairing the new Better Pay and Benefits Task Force launched by the Congressional Progressive Caucus to sharpen our messaging around good jobs, wages, and cost of living ahead of the 2026 elections. [_**We win when we put working people first – read more →**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/112233208/559841531/1135692330?ID=4B8C27EA-46D9-4A70-99E3-AEB8C99ECA43&refcode=RS_EM_EN_2025.07.25_JulyNewsletter_Actives&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwMDJkYWY5MS0xMDZiLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=LMYInMcmtIUewl8pR64M6a_Y0AKe-jtUK_A4vmJCUqw=&emci=f4a4d933-d568-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&emdi=002daf91-106b-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&ceid=23483442) • **Disability Pride Month** – July marks 35 years since Congress passed the landmark Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA), legislation that’s helped millions of Americans live and thrive with dignity, including my sister, Olivia. _**[I shared my sister’s story on the House floor →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/112233210/559841532/-1942685653?hl=en&refcode=RS_EM_EN_2025.07.25_JulyNewsletter_Actives&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwMDJkYWY5MS0xMDZiLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=LMYInMcmtIUewl8pR64M6a_Y0AKe-jtUK_A4vmJCUqw=&emci=f4a4d933-d568-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&emdi=002daf91-106b-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&ceid=23483442) **_ • **A league of our own** – The women of Congress (of both parties) competed against members of the press in this year’s Congressional Softball Game. The game raises money for the Young Survival Coalition, a global organization dedicated to helping young adults who are diagnosed with breast cancer. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/112233212/559841534/711325247?hl=en&img_index=1&refcode=RS_EM_EN_2025.07.25_JulyNewsletter_Actives&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwMDJkYWY5MS0xMDZiLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=LMYInMcmtIUewl8pR64M6a_Y0AKe-jtUK_A4vmJCUqw=&emci=f4a4d933-d568-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&emdi=002daf91-106b-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&ceid=23483442) • **The Big Bad Bill** – Thank you again to everyone who called and sent messages to our office with your stories about how Medicaid and SNAP have impacted your life. clean_body: Welcome to the latest edition of Emily’s Notes — updates from Washington’s 6th and Washington, D.C.! | Peter – Welcome to the latest edition of Emily’s Notes — updates from Washington’s 6th and Washington, D.C.! Here’s what I’ve been up to: • First, some positive news – We’re bringing $18 million home to Washington’s 6th District for infrastructure improvements, expanded access to health care, and public safety initiatives! These funded projects cover all six counties in the 6th District – from building a shelter for severe weather events in Clallam County to updating outdated health care records systems in Grays Harbor County. • Cleanup at Indian Creek – Governor Ferguson and I visited the site where a fuel tanker overturned at Indian Creek, threatening the Elwha River and drinking water for the city of Port Angeles. We’ll work with state, local, and Tribal leaders to clean our river and protect our drinking water. • Providing an ANCHOR – I grew up around many military families and know the difficulties faced during relocation. I introduced bipartisan legislation to address those issues: the Assuring Navigation of Compact Help For Ongoing Relocation (ANCHOR) for Military Families Act. • You deserve a raise – I’ll be chairing the new Better Pay and Benefits Task Force launched by the Congressional Progressive Caucus to sharpen our messaging around good jobs, wages, and cost of living ahead of the 2026 elections. • Disability Pride Month – July marks 35 years since Congress passed the landmark Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA), legislation that’s helped millions of Americans live and thrive with dignity, including my sister, Olivia. • A league of our own – The women of Congress (of both parties) competed against members of the press in this year’s Congressional Softball Game. The game raises money for the Young Survival Coalition, a global organization dedicated to helping young adults who are diagnosed with breast cancer. • The Big Bad Bill – Thank you again to everyone who called and sent messages to our office with your stories about how Medicaid and SNAP have impacted your life. I brought your stories to the , where I tried to get Medicaid cuts removed from the GOP budget, aka the Big Bad Bill. (Republicans voted NO.) Then, , I offered an amendment to remove all Medicaid and SNAP cuts from the final version of the bill. (Republicans refused to hear it.) The bill narrowly passed. Now is the part where I ask you for a donation. The passage of the Big Bad Bill was a reminder – we can tell your stories, bring in experts, and do everything we can to slow down a bad bill. But at the end of the day, the GOP had more votes. Trump and Republicans are terrified of losing the House. (That’s why they are desperate to redraw the district maps!) And the more we can do now to support candidates in swing districts, the closer we’ll be to victory. = = Thank you so much 💙 – Emily = | | Paid for by Emily Randall for Congress Emily Randall for Congress P.O. Box 1883 Port Orchard, WA 98366 United States | | If you would like to receive only our most important messages, . name: Marcy Kaptur
subject: Local wins really matter
body:
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP33/1/95655/images/kaptur-email-header-2.png]

Team, the district that I represent in Congress is my lifelong home. I grew up here, I still live here, and I know our Northwest Ohio community as well as anybody.I know the struggles that Ohio families are facing — because they're the same issues that matter to my friends, my neighbors, and my family.

So when we deliver major wins for OH-09, I know those aren't just numbers on a spreadsheet. These wins represent transformative investments in our schools, our hospitals, our infrastructure, and our people.

Last year, we brought home over $700 million in federal investments to our district. Those victories make a real difference in people's lives. They paid for...

🎓 New educational facilities

🧬 Cutting-edge research

✈️ Improvements to our railways, airports, cities, and bridges

💧 Conservation of our Great Lakes ecosystem

⚙️ Revitalizing Ohio manufacturing

⚕️ Investments in quality, affordable healthcare

🚀 Support for our military and aerospace facilities

...and so much more.

Friend, wins like these are the reason that I fight harder for our district than anybody. These victories matter. I'll never stop fighting for you. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kaptur25_em?refcode=250727_mk_local]

I'm grateful to have you with me in that fight, friend.

CHIP IN [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kaptur25_em?refcode=250727_mk_local_b1]Thank you for your support.

— Marcy
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP33/1/95655/images/kaptur-email-headshot.jpg]

About Marcy Kaptur
Marcy Kaptur is a fighter for high-wage jobs, growing a workforce to meet the needs of the 21st century, and protecting our region’s most vital natural resource, the Great Lakes. She’s worked with both parties to secure investments in our communities and has been a true friend of veterans, seniors, first responders and working people. She’s a voice for us all in the People’s House.
Donate Now [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kaptur25_em?refcode=emailfooter&refcode2=f57140f3-126b-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b] Give Monthly [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kaptur25_em?recurring=true&refcode=footermonthly&refcode2=f57140f3-126b-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b]
Follow Marcy
https://twitter.com/Marcy_Kaptur [https://twitter.com/Marcy_Kaptur] https://www.facebook.com/marcykapturforcongress [https://www.facebook.com/marcykapturforcongress]
Checks may also be mailed to:
Kaptur for Congress - PO Box 899, Toledo, OH 43697 Paid for by Kaptur for Congress Email is a critical way to stay in touch with our supporters, but if you wish to unsubscribe, you may do so here [https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/tcAb-PaXtEayL-mWUU_QGw2?unsubscribedata=8ud0SIs6DA4%2FVhhjxUjMXsLe%2FhDb6jqVPWrBFI8MEnzFgGya79kv22uta3Js%2FL4UvsaSr0XL7ezoUhjDeX5vu7E%2FJtN%2BzLQVWzi%2F%2BGqukFI6p4xMMB6RI9bL%2BM50k3F4ZFL8adEeKYKoThrpYI3ex%2B6qFXuviN389618MMPzAm%2BgxcwIGjOzUSk6XCZup6EAw5tha45MYv%2BOL6t6MisQB4QW95e2GrXcTccks5wrEKi0RbsCnYgCvMV6Pz0luocfFoWctK%2F8Qkj9JXiPE8%2FiLPr5ukLknULuWFcyn9CrRadWzIn%2FFHXyxf6RkwntKPowEpraHoAZnaua%2Bs4xE5GvUiHGWyKd40OaoQOefwic6ch10Cjx7H%2BDfUGEgyN0OfiSO28EFfRCupRiwj1iFPDCXI5zO7fKar4V2Q1OksiARrs%3D] . Toledo, OH 43697

Every single day from now until November 4 matters, and I need YOUR help to defeat Bruce Blakeman in November.

Peter —

Sundays tend to be the worst day of the week to reach out, so we appreciate you taking the time to read this.

It’s the middle of summer, folks are rarely checking their inboxes, and it’s hard not to notice the impact this is having on our numbers. **Because if we’re talking numbers... our end of month fundraising deadline is just 4 days away!** [To keep momentum high, we’ve set a goal to raise $10,000 before midnight on the 31st. We know $10 might not seem like much in comparison to the GOP's millions, but a little truly goes a long way, Peter. If you join the network of grassroots folks who have already given $10 this week, we’ll have the resources it takes to fight back and defend Valerie’s seat.

name: Justice Democrats
subject: 100+ Dems helped pass massive crypto giveaways
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

Peter,

To conclude “Crypto Week” in Congress, more than 100 Democrats joined
Republicans in passing two massive giveaways to the corporate-backed
Crypto lobby.

With pressure from Democratic sell-outs like Josh Gottheimer, Ritchie
Torres, and Don Davis — whose campaigns have collectively received
millions in donations from the crypto lobby — 78 Democrats joined every
Republican to vote for the CLARITY Act, with even more (102 Democrats)
voting for the GENIUS Act.

Justice Democrats like Reps. Ayanna Presley and Delia Ramirez loudly
opposed these bills and exposed them for what they really are: a green
light to self-enriching crypto schemes.

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250727?refcode=em20250727-36881&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&refcode2=36881_1203867_qqXFxl&akid=36881%2E1203867%2EqqXFxl ]Tweet from Congresswoman Delia C. Ramirez: Republicans want to force 3
bills on crypto through the House that reduce regulations, weaken
enforcement, erode consumer protections, empower corporate giants & big
banks, threaten our national security, and allow Trump to enrich himself.
I am a HELL NO.

Instead of stopping Trump’s personal crypto grift and properly regulating
the industry, these bills endanger consumers, open the floodgates for even
more fraud and even less oversight to boost corporate profits, and make
crypto billionaires even richer — all while allowing a taxpayer-funded
bailout when crypto inevitably fails.

In summary: They profit, we pay.

The passage of these bills comes after pro-crypto super PACs spent more
than $170 million to influence elections last cycle. The crypto lobby
effectively bought this legislation,
Peter.

By becoming the biggest corporate spender in the 2024 election cycle, Big
Crypto secured enough votes on both sides of the aisle to give Silicon
Valley and Wall Street new ways to get richer by endangering your life
savings and our economy.

More than 100 corporate Democrats threw working-class people under the bus
and legitimized Trump’s crypto scheme by supporting these bills. They
don’t deserve their seats in Congress, and they need to be primaried in
2026.

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250727?refcode=em20250727-36881&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&refcode2=36881_1203867_qqXFxl&akid=36881%2E1203867%2EqqXFxl ]We’re actively recruiting progressive, working-class candidates to
challenge out-of-touch, corporate Democrats who put crypto profits before
the people. Fuel our fight with $5, $10, or whatever works for you today →

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250727?refcode=em20250727-36881&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&refcode2=36881_1203867_qqXFxl&akid=36881%2E1203867%2EqqXFxl ]Contribute $5

The Crypto lobby poses a grave threat not only to our economy but our
democracy as we know it. That’s why we need to rally and fight back NOW
before it’s too late.

In solidarity,

Justice Democrats

Contribute to Justice Democrats: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20250727-36881-footer&t=1002&refcode2=36881_1203867_qqXFxl&akid=36881%2E1203867%2EqqXFxl

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://go.justicedemocrats.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1003&akid=36881%2E1203867%2EqqXFxl

PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee.
Justice Democrats
1390 Columbia Ave #261
Lancaster PA 17603
Email us: us@justicedemocrats.com
