name: Christina Bohannan
subject: I’ve waited tables, cleaned trailers, and picked oranges
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY CHRISTINA BOHANNAN FOR CONGRESS
body:
Every job I’ve had has taught me something different.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/112236261/559852226/-975735282?refcode=em_logoheader&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjIyOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjYjYzZmFlOC0xYTZiLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=gzCxFpixTDp9dUkIi1d8PyWvvJ9P0c0lRQvTfwZu170=)
Hi Peter, Christina here.

There’s a lot you might already know about me, but what you might _not_ know is the path I took to get here.

I grew up in a working-class family. My dad was a construction worker, and my mom cared for kids at a daycare. We didn’t have much, but they showed me what it means to take pride in your work — and to never give up on the things that matter, even when the going gets tough. I’ve carried that lesson with me every step of the way.

Over the years, I’ve waited tables, cleaned trailers, and picked oranges. I worked my way through college as an environmental engineer, where I inspected drinking water and sewage treatment plants (the smell was just as bad as you’d imagine). Eventually, I earned a law degree and became a professor at the University of Iowa (Go Hawkeyes!), where I now teach students about the Constitution and the importance of protecting our democracy.

**Every job I’ve had has taught me something different and introduced me to people from all walks of life. But the common thread through them all is this: I _know_ what it’s like to work hard. I know what it means to earn a living. And I know how it feels to support your family while chasing a better future.**

I'm running for Congress because this district deserves someone who understands the struggles of working families and will _actually_ show up for them. I’ve never been afraid of hard work, and I promise I will hit the ground running in Washington.

**[But I can’t do it without you. This campaign is fueled by grassroots supporters like you — not corporate PACs or wealthy donors. Will you chip in today to help power our grassroots campaign forward before our end-of-month fundraising deadline?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/112236262/559852227/-1612730695?refcode=em25_250727_EOM1_fnd_gq_act___CB_ettx&utm_campaign=em25_250727_EOM1_fnd_gq_act___CB_ettx&utm_medium=email&refcode2=cb63fae8-1a6b-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjIyOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjYjYzZmFlOC0xYTZiLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=gzCxFpixTDp9dUkIi1d8PyWvvJ9P0c0lRQvTfwZu170=)**

clean_body: Every job I’ve had has taught me something different. | = Hi Peter, Christina here. There’s a lot you might already know about me, but what you might not know is the path I took to get here. I grew up in a working-class family. My dad was a construction worker, and my mom cared for kids at a daycare. We didn’t have much, but they showed me what it means to take pride in your work — and to never give up on the things that matter, even when the going gets tough. I’ve carried that lesson with me every step of the way. Over the years, I’ve waited tables, cleaned trailers, and picked oranges. I worked my way through college as an environmental engineer, where I inspected drinking water and sewage treatment plants (the smell was just as bad as you’d imagine). Eventually, I earned a law degree and became a professor at the University of Iowa (Go Hawkeyes!), where I now teach students about the Constitution and the importance of protecting our democracy. Every job I’ve had has taught me something different and introduced me to people from all walks of life. But the common thread through them all is this: I know what it’s like to work hard. I know what it means to earn a living. And I know how it feels to support your family while chasing a better future. I'm running for Congress because this district deserves someone who understands the struggles of working families and will actually show up for them. I’ve never been afraid of hard work, and I promise I will hit the ground running in Washington. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through clean_body: | = ➤➤ NEW: States file lawsuit to stop Trump’s election power grab = This is urgent, friend. A GOP group is suing to force the state of Illinois to PURGE its voter rolls. Their argument? That Illinois isn’t kicking enough people off the rolls – and the DOJ just backed their central claim. Horrifying. If they win in Illinois, they’ll copy-paste this strategy across every state in the country. Voter by voter. County by county. This isn’t about “election integrity.” It’s about rigging the rules so MAGA Republicans have an edge – no matter how many voters they have to remove to do it. Our Democratic Secretaries of State are standing up and speaking out against these barrage of attacks. name: David Hogg
subject: This is about the generation we represent
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY LEADERS WE DESERVE, LEADERSWEDESERVE
body:
We can only keep up the fight with your support.
# [](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/852?refcode=em20250727-1&t=1&akid=2441%2E587540%2EZA8tzV)
Peter, I hear the same kind of frustrated reaction to Democratic fundraising a lot:
**_“Why are you asking me for $5 when Trump is destroying our country and Democrats aren’t doing anything about it?”_**
I can’t speak for every group, but I do know what sets Leaders We Deserve apart.
We represent a generation of people who walked out of their schools, marching with millions of their peers to demand action on gun violence and mass shootings.
Young people are at the forefront of progressive movements, fighting for solutions on issues like gun reform, climate change, student debt, and Gaza, to name a few.
Now, these same young leaders are old enough to actually run for political office, right at the time when we need a massive movement of young people in power.
**Leaders We Deserve exists to transform these movements into political power.** Running for office is more expensive than ever — and these young leaders need our support to help lead their movements into state legislatures and Congress. We know that the billionaire class isn’t showing up to bankroll these campaigns, so it’s not an exaggeration to say they can’t win these fights without grassroots support.
**[Peter, can you contribute $5 to power our transformational movement?](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_lwd_20250727_1_transformational?refcode=em20250727-2441&amount=5&refcode2=2441_587540_ZA8tzV&akid=2441%2E587540%2EZA8tzV)**
**The young candidates that Leaders We Deserve supports are gamechangers. They will revitalize the Democratic Party and revolutionize our government.**
Our candidates are leading the fight to rebuild our political system. And they aren’t rebuilding the same political system that brought us school shootings, climate change, and everything that younger generations have to deal with in America.
They’re building something better — a system that actually _works_ for the people.
I believe that a big part of the answer to Donald Trump is building a movement of young people who will take back the reins of our government from the far right.
**Young people are up to this challenge.** If history has taught us anything, it's that great generations aren’t born, they’re made in the crucible of awful circumstances.
So when you give a donation to Leaders We Deserve, you aren’t just throwing out money at the status quo. You are investing in a different vision for what’s possible.
**[Please — pitch in $5 now to join our movement and fight for our future.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_lwd_20250727_1_transformational?refcode=em20250727-2441&amount=5&refcode2=2441_587540_ZA8tzV&akid=2441%2E587540%2EZA8tzV)**
We can only do this work with your support. I deeply appreciate your generosity.
Thank you,
David Hogg
* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lwd-em-footer?refcode=em20250727-1&t=2&refcode2=2441_587540_ZA8tzV&akid=2441%2E587540%2EZA8tzV)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=3&akid=2441%2E587540%2EZA8tzV)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/signup/update-your-information/)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=4&akid=2441%2E587540%2EZA8tzV)

To give by check:
Leaders We Deserve
611 Pennsylvania Ave SE
#192
Washington, DC 20003
Email us: [info@leaderswedeserve.com](mailto:info@leaderswedeserve.com)
PAID FOR BY LEADERS WE DESERVE, LEADERSWEDESERVE.COM, NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE OR CANDIDATE'S COMMITTEE

Vice President JD Vance grew up in a blue-collar community, like mine, so he knows that working people often rely on Medicaid and SNAP. **That's why it's all the more disturbing to see him claim that his administration's cuts don't matter.**
My experience growing up around families who relied on Medicaid and SNAP to make ends meet turned me into a fighter for the working class. I went into public service because I wanted to make my community better.
I know firsthand just how critical those programs are, and so does Vance. But Vance has made his motives clear, and he'd rather rub shoulders with billionaires in Washington than fight for the American people.
**There's just no excuse for the real pain that these cuts will cause across our Commonwealth. **
Rural hospitals will be forced to close their doors. Everyone, from farmers to grocers, utilizes SNAP to keep their businesses afloat. People across Pennsylvania and the nation will be forced to go without the care they need. Even if you don't use Medicaid or SNAP directly, these cuts will impact you.

**I believe that those who are closest to the pain should be closest to the power. We're seeing right now what happens when those who are furthest from the pain have all the power in Washington. It's stealing from the poor to give to the rich. **
[**We cannot let their power continue unchecked, which is why I’m asking for your help today. Will you chip in to my re-election campaign now so I can keep standing up for Pennsylvanians?** ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/112237687/559857275/160205011?amount=10&source=SBD_AD_EM_FR_072725_FWDVANCE_ATO_D1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NzIyMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4NTcxZjkzNy0yMDZiLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=fEILX6K2zyxar3gYhPKxOqBn1kkhBW1P3k4TraWE7RA=)

clean_body: Austin Davis is helping continue the progress we've made for Pennsylvanians by ensuring we keep Pennsylvania blue up and down the ballot. If you’d like to unsubscribe, | | = Peter, it's Lt. Gov. Austin Davis. Recently, I wrote a guest column in The Times Leader about JD Vance's unwelcome visit to the Commonwealth. In case you missed it, here's the gist: What Congressional Republicans in Washington voted for will make life even harder for blue-collar and working-class people, all to give tax breaks to their billionaire friends. JD Vance should be ashamed of what he did to Pennsylvanians and hardworking people across the country. = | | = If you want to know more about my thoughts on the Vice President's visit, you can read my original email below ⬇️ \---------- Forwarded message --------- From: Austin Davis Subject: An unwelcome visit from our Vice President To: dpwillis67@gmail.com JD Vance decided to show up in Pennsylvania in an attempt to distract us from the fact that he just passed a historically awful spending bill that will rob millions of their healthcare coverage and food assistance, all to pay for tax breaks for the ultra-rich. = | = Vice President JD Vance grew up in a blue-collar community, like mine, so he knows that working people often rely on Medicaid and SNAP. That's why it's all the more disturbing to see him claim that his administration's cuts don't matter. My experience growing up around families who relied on Medicaid and SNAP to make ends meet turned me into a fighter for the working class. I went into public service because I wanted to make my community better. I know firsthand just how critical those programs are, and so does Vance. But Vance has made his motives clear, and he'd rather rub shoulders with billionaires in Washington than fight for the American people. There's just no excuse for the real pain that these cuts will cause across our Commonwealth. Rural hospitals will be forced to close their doors. Everyone, from farmers to grocers, utilizes SNAP to keep their businesses afloat. People across Pennsylvania and the nation will be forced to go without the care they need. Even if you don't use Medicaid or SNAP directly, these cuts will impact you. I believe that those who are closest to the pain should be closest to the power. We're seeing right now what happens when those who are furthest from the pain have all the power in Washington. It's stealing from the poor to give to the rich. = If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process now. | = | = | = | = | = | = Thank you, Peter. = Lt. Gov. Republican groups raised a record-breaking $60 million in just the first half of this year.
That’s not a typo. **$60 million.**
And because southern New Mexico is one of their top targets, you can bet a big chunk of that money is headed straight here.
On top of that, we’re behind on our projection for July by over $10,000. Now’s the time when our fundraising tends to hit the “summer slump” — and if we don’t catch up before the end of the month, Republicans will get the opening they need to flip this seat red.

subject: Peter, we need ALL good Democrats to take action NOW
body:

name: Brent Hennrich
subject: She took $42K from landlords while you struggle to afford rent
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Brent Hennrich for Washington
body:
| | [ ](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMU_BeZcmRBr16dPjlrMKhhjiC51ataw3Peft1wIO87ITHybou7O2Ov3kEb9C24dC1G3YT3WVWuyo_hd6JHefCLYHGWFi4U0Zjh1ZVrL3UyD5r27qhKbvZF3XNdkOWXWu8S_AJLliIpRfdL3feJB1ziQyvYXQ2nySqeU4YoSQwwNKfG8mwR0BJRT-x6v15w3bg3c4eM1Sgt3vNWxOBu8qJBYRbFti31D2Rq7SaFwSOekUbDeakbWswwitY6BWLru8gUT1dxIl7v09fyHDwmMt8Uk/4ij/74CjiWQxQWqLBk6tg9vDCQ/h0/h001.iiaP-KY6AyfPK4EmXFNdnlbJLfflNLSi0EP_Mzmz4bY)
---
Peter,

The dream of owning a home in America is being crushed right now for millions of people.

It's not just rent that's going up, the entire system is rigged. Wall Street, private equity firms, hedge funds, and corporate landlords are treating our homes like cash machines instead of homes where families belong.

Guess who's taking money from them.
That's right: Marie Gluesenkamp Perez took over **$42,000** last cycle from finance and real estate interests.

Her second-biggest donor group isn't renters or working families, it's corporate landlords and real estate PACs. So if you're wondering why nothing's getting done to make housing affordable, look no further than who's funding her.

In some parts of the country, people making six figures now qualify for low-income housing. That's not a problem with the programs, it's a problem because corporate landlords are jacking up prices beyond reason.

**Marie hasn 't sponsored a single major bill to stop rent gouging or fight back for her constituents.

**She has a choice--stand with struggling renters and working families or side with the slumlords. She chooses Wall Street every time. Help me get to Congress so I can put a stop to this grift.
---
[ **DONATE TODAY** ](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqv6r8GR7zKbLdKY8JcRsBH8aQhQzL-6XFLiJ1IzrYZqHcVu3_2xEWSSvdjjDmkiSUCFy642MrkbdIZU1JtdfmayaBDpqJ7_ksB-abKTviNBXLhdKxXhK9OnNVGb2bpN0F7zuAeJeMNw6utRrlZiME3YwrILD_w4Ly2vFsmlpfFwPxUqrLVozVC_t_hlUnOz50Hc6ybfqKKCwGwhrLOPXVy8ulLXrUez0OYW7fYGHgfMYWsjlp-31SWYOSB04tlUvuYdCe64KIkr3hT6jIxCZECw_oDhSeCLEGPqA8H93Nn_as6ffQdtbi9dLU43ciyNXAT-WEnQ0WEn4mGLJ9uOM2cHUSMRoaHEIRWZoG9cpb2YCOVFbLq2rxFjn9A74bHVNxGM5j7Vip5eY3Ik1K0a6c9h/4ij/74CjiWQxQWqLBk6tg9vDCQ/h1/h001.NMLnJqXGnwHd_bft4tGU6GIbJ-ZcHr1bnx5bW8Mwe60)
---
This isn't just failure. It's complicity. A decade of deregulation and greed, with politicians like Marie cashing checks while pretending to give a damn.

I'll fight like hell to fix this! From sponsoring down payment assistance, changes to FHA, and permit cost buydown, we can make home ownership affordable and attainable.

But I can't do it without you.

[**Chip in $10, $25, or $50 right now to help me take on Wall Street 's grip on housing and build a future where working people get a fair shot at a place to call home.

**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqv6r8GR7zKbLdKY8JcRsBH8aQhQzL-6XFLiJ1IzrYZqHcVu3_2xEWSSvdjjDmkiSUCFy642MrkbdIZU1JtdfmayaBDpqJ7_ksB-abKTviNBXLhdKxXhK9OnNVGb2bpN0F5s2wDYWEJldb3NIpkRhSy4WPZ3ww2_ToZ8AQK_Ixv7oVPcX5f0Kyn5kb_iZRPcSw0yLHUGm_iqfehJPygjH473Sh0n2Wb8h2HtGzdXNX5joBvDUQ8TcDzc8H-XD3aXEEfCuWyNQvvWUCkf_3oWlDCCgoa5xVs-Gh_lbKuh4RR_4TeCL7B9KDvfvKgdpbAGCorpidE87Bt-P8hKDPJLNWvB4INVnkrot-q3FOEpMJp6vZY1ssNUsLy_zpBYUGuHvRq4WQ6pdIs6vM4oa4szGfVg/4ij/74CjiWQxQWqLBk6tg9vDCQ/h2/h001.Puz2sqUU5FEFbgf2oHaJWOdqQWwdG76_DHG6Jbf8JNY)We deserve better than a Democrat who's bought and sold by the landlords. We deserve someone who fights for us.

Let's Heal Washington!

Brent Hennrich

📣 He told the nation he’ll be a “dictator on day one.”
📣 He’s promising mass arrests, loyalty purges, and unchecked executive power.
📣 And MAGA Republicans are cheering him on.

**This is NOT normal – but it will become the norm if we don’t fight back.**

That’s why Democrats are rallying behind the **NO KINGS ACT** – to rein in executive overreach, prevent authoritarianism, and make it clear: no president is above the law.

This is NOT normal – but it will become the norm if we don’t fight back. That’s why Democrats are rallying behind the NO KINGS ACT – to rein in executive overreach, prevent authoritarianism, and make it clear: no president is above the law. * * *
_We are being governed by a mad man._ Donald Trump, a convicted felon and adjudicated sex offender, spirals further into madness as he desperately attempts to dodge accountability for his shadowy past with the notorious sex trafficker, the late Jeffrey Epstein. Promises to release the Epstein files remain unfulfilled, as Trump deploys his FBI enforcers—1,000 strong—to scrub any mention of him from these damning documents. As this egregious cover-up unfolds, Trump takes to social media with increasingly bizarre posts, attempting to normalize the idea of arresting political foes without cause. Trump’s audacity knows no bounds, even posting an AI-generated video depicting former President Barack Obama being unjustly arrested. **The stakes couldn’t be higher. As long as Donald Trump clings to power in the White House, our nation teeters on the brink of chaos and corruption.** [**Help us push back against this depravity and preserve our democracy. clean_body: | | | ** Donald J. Trump is NOT ok. We are being governed by a mad man. Donald Trump, a convicted felon and adjudicated sex offender, spirals further into madness as he desperately attempts to dodge accountability for his shadowy past with the notorious sex trafficker, the late Jeffrey Epstein. Promises to release the Epstein files remain unfulfilled, as Trump deploys his FBI enforcers—1,000 strong—to scrub any mention of him from these damning documents. As this egregious cover-up unfolds, Trump takes to social media with increasingly bizarre posts, attempting to normalize the idea of arresting political foes without cause. Trump’s audacity knows no bounds, even posting an AI-generated video depicting former President Barack Obama being unjustly arrested. The stakes couldn’t be higher. As long as Donald Trump clings to power in the White House, our nation teeters on the brink of chaos and corruption. Peter, it’s Charlie. We don’t have a shortage of MAGA Republicans in Georgia. And we certainly don’t have a shortage of Republicans who think they deserve a reward for doing nothing for the people they claim to represent. Buddy Carter. Burt Jones. Chris Carr. Marjorie Taylor Greene. They’ve always put Trump first, billionaires and big corporations second — and hardworking Georgians dead last. But this is also the state that gave us Stacey Abrams, Rev. Raphael Warnock, Sen. Jon Ossoff — a President for the people in Jimmy Carter, and a civil rights giant in John Lewis. Georgia is capable of achieving great things. And come 2026, the people of Georgia will once again choose progress over extremism. But we can’t take anything for granted. Peter, we need your help to lay the groundwork for critical gains across Georgia: to protect our Senate seat, elect proven Democratic champions, and move this state forward, not backwards. **It’s time to turn Georgia from a purple state to a blue stronghold once and for all.** But none of that works without you. And we certainly don’t have a shortage of Republicans who think they deserve a reward for doing nothing for the people they claim to represent. Buddy Carter. Burt Jones. Chris Carr. Marjorie Taylor Greene. They’ve always put Trump first, billionaires and big corporations second — and hardworking Georgians dead last. But this is also the state that gave us Stacey Abrams, Rev. Raphael Warnock, Sen. Jon Ossoff — a President for the people in Jimmy Carter, and a civil rights giant in John Lewis. Georgia is capable of achieving great things. And come 2026, the people of Georgia will once again choose progress over extremism. But we can’t take anything for granted. Peter, we need your help to lay the groundwork for critical gains across Georgia: to protect our Senate seat, elect proven Democratic champions, and move this state forward, not backwards. body:

subject: Do you watch PBS?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by State Democrats Committee (StateDemocrats
body:
Millions of Americans rely on their local PBS station for news, education programming, and so much more.
#

**Do you watch PBS?** | [YES, I WATCH PBS](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/92697?t=1&akid=9173%2E614464%2E-BdjJ-) [NO, I DON’T WATCH PBS](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/92697?t=2&akid=9173%2E614464%2E-BdjJ-)
---
Here’s what happened, Peter:
**FIRST —** Donald Trump + MAGA Republicans in Congress announced their effort to cut more than a BILLION DOLLARS from PBS + NPR.
**THEN —** They actually did it. Earlier this month, they officially cut $1.1 BILLION in federal funding for public broadcasters on radio + TV across the country.
**NOW — These cuts mean that PBS + NPR stations, especially in rural areas, are at risk of shuttering.**
Millions of Americans rely on their local PBS station for news, educational programming, and so much more. **So today, we’re asking you to confirm with us:**
**Do you watch PBS?**
[YES, I WATCH PBS](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/92697?t=3&akid=9173%2E614464%2E-BdjJ-) [NO, I DON’T WATCH PBS](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/92697?t=4&akid=9173%2E614464%2E-BdjJ-)

name: Team Fight Like Hell PAC
subject: While Republicans go on summer break, we’re holding them accountable
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Fight Like Hell PAC
body:
We’re launching a $10,000 push before the end of July, will you help? |
#

Team, **Mike Johnson and House Republicans just ended their Congressional session early and headed home for August recess.** But not before ramming through devastating cuts to Medicaid and critical programs families depend on. They cut health care. They cut SNAP. They cut support for working families. _And now they’re taking a summer break?_ **While they’re at home, we’re not going to let up.** That’s why **Fight Like Hell PAC is launching a $10,000 end-of-month fundraising push to hold Republicans accountable** and tell voters exactly where their representatives stand: with billionaires and special interests, not the people they were elected to serve. **[We need your help to hit our goal and keep up the pressure. clean_body: We’re launching a $10,000 push before the end of July, will you help? | Team, Mike Johnson and House Republicans just ended their Congressional session early and headed home for August recess. But not before ramming through devastating cuts to Medicaid and critical programs families depend on. They cut health care. They cut SNAP. They cut support for working families. And now they’re taking a summer break? While they’re at home, we’re not going to let up. That’s why Fight Like Hell PAC is launching a $10,000 end-of-month fundraising push to hold Republicans accountable and tell voters exactly where their representatives stand: with billionaires and special interests, not the people they were elected to serve. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | = = | = = | = = We’re not taking any breaks when Michiganders are under attack. _Democrat Eric Swalwell represents California's 14th Congressional District. _
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/112239344/559862610/-1590518309?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS85MDcyNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjNWNhYzA2YS0yYTZiLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=jUZ3_EpuoEGLcVT4OX68CIPB2FtIWigmz9ZbYcQQW24=&emci=f492fb4e-c568-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&emdi=c5cac06a-2a6b-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&ceid=7878003)
They want us to quit, friend. I’ve spent countless hours fighting back against their attacks in DC, on the ground in California, and shining a light on their BS, and I can promise you one thing: **they want us to give up. ** And that is _exactly why I’m not gonna quit. _ I’ve seen what the GOP is doing up close: deporting sick kids, tearing families apart, letting ICE lawlessly run all over our cities, gleefully kicking millions of people off their health care, and giving Trump anything he wants any time he asks. **And they are trying to exhaust us - it’s why we cannot quit.[Not now, not ever - but I need your help to stay in the fight.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/112239345/559862611/444482356?&refcode=E14299-20250727-EOM2-SL-DeadlineEOM&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS85MDcyNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjNWNhYzA2YS0yYTZiLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=jUZ3_EpuoEGLcVT4OX68CIPB2FtIWigmz9ZbYcQQW24=)** Look, being in the minority means one of the loudest tools we have is speaking up, calling them out on their BS, and I am making sure that I am doing every single thing I can to keep you informed about what they’re trying to do to the American people. But if we want to hold them accountable, **we have to win back the House next year.[And that is going to take meeting every goal we set, and making sure we have the money we need to get the American people to turn out and WIN.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/112239346/559862612/444482356?&refcode=E14299-20250727-EOM2-SL-DeadlineEOM&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS85MDcyNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjNWNhYzA2YS0yYTZiLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=jUZ3_EpuoEGLcVT4OX68CIPB2FtIWigmz9ZbYcQQW24=)** | _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_

All the hate we received only served to shine a greater spotlight on the move of the Gospel happening in Canada! Canadian Christians are tired of government persecution—and they’re not staying silent. Tonight, at 6:30 EDT, at our last revival stop on the Eastern Canadian tour, I’m addressing Canada and telling both government officials and Believers alike what they can expect moving forward. _**STREAM THE ADDRESS TO CANADA ⬇︎⬇︎⬇︎**_

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/5a6f717d-e2b3-4824-84ac-8f44ee7acfc5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
In my home state of New Hampshire, July can get hot. While it's perhaps not as frequent as you might experience in Louisiana, there are lots of days we suffer a wet, sticky kind of hot that makes your clothes stick to you and makes it difficult to breathe. And as anyone from Las Vegas can attest, while heat can be uncomfortable, heat and humidity can be unbearable.
And it's this humid heat that contributed to my recent decision to install a nitrogen tunnel at the pizza factory I now run. By spraying liquid nitrogen, we're able to rapidly cool hot products - a functionality that enhances food safety, food quality, and our ability to operate during hot, humid New Hampshire summer days. This has proven especially important (and useful) over the past month when the temperature on the factory floor has soared on several days. If we didn't have the ability to use liquid nitrogen, we would likely have been forced to shut down production on those 90 degree plus days.
* * *
## [Zigging While Others Zag: Investing to Cool a Hot Market](https://substack.com/redirect/49f85cb3-c991-4e70-962c-a9c6282303d9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
---
| | [Vikram Mansharamani](https://substack.com/profile/37278065-vikram-mansharamani)| | *
---|---|---
| Jun 14
[](https://substack.com/redirect/49f85cb3-c991-4e70-962c-a9c6282303d9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[For many business leaders, the cross currents of a global trade war, military conflict in the Middle East, shifting geopolitical pressures, and disruption from new technologies have created a paralyz...](https://substack.com/redirect/49f85cb3-c991-4e70-962c-a9c6282303d9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
| [| Read full story
---](https://substack.com/redirect/49f85cb3-c991-4e70-962c-a9c6282303d9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
All of this got me thinking about air conditioning, and in particular the differing attitudes held by Americans and non-Americans about our use of energy to cool our living spaces. In India, where rolling blackouts led to on again, off again air conditioning, I recall watching swings of almost 35 degrees on the thermostat one August almost a decade ago. I've also found myself struggling to sleep in emerging market countries as varied as Ghana, Malaysia, Uruguay, and Pakistan. These travels always made me feel grateful to live in a country that had regular electricity and _could afford_ air conditioning. Even if money couldn't buy happiness, it could buy comfort...or so I believed.
Turns out I was wrong; _it 's not just about money_. The politics of air conditioning are heating up and nowhere is this dynamic more on display than in Europe, a part of the world that should be able to afford comfort. While a recent heat wave is the nominal cause, there are deeper tensions to consider, as noted in a recent _Wall Street Journal_ [article](https://substack.com/redirect/4b385209-a8e3-45a9-902c-e90609e22699?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Air conditioning has emerged as a political football in the scrum between European conservatives and liberals.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..xtrU5a4Y7X70JGAqZSO8q-1drsh9dW_0rDyZoiHzAvg?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=169388892)
In the United Kingdom, conservatives called for the easing of regulations that make it hard for air conditioning to be installed in new housing; and in Spain, the right-wing Vox party has been attacking the country's mainstream parties as failing to protect people from the heat. And in France, populist Marine Le Pen has called for an [urgent "national air conditioning plan" in response to more than 2,000 heat related school closures](https://substack.com/redirect/96ccf0f2-9824-4267-94d8-cbbcbb94cc00?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and apparently problematic conditions in nursing homes.
All of this had generated political backlash, mainly from those in power and environmentalists. The _[Wall Street Journal noted](https://substack.com/redirect/39dd7d19-283d-46b2-b0ee-200d97bdabb8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_ that, in France, "Energy minister Agnes Pannier-Runacher said large-scale air conditioning would heat up streets with the machines' exhaust, making heat waves worse" and went on to note she was "flanked by the perspiring prime minister, François Bayrou." (Note: sweating French Prime Ministers are not a new phenomenon in France, see picture below, taken in 2015). The French media also joined the fight against air conditioning, warning that going from an air-conditioned room to the hot outdoors could trigger "thermal shock, resulting in nausea, loss of consciousness, and even respiratory arrest."
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/ae903ec3-091b-4137-89cc-a63deb08d787?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Source: [https://www.mirror.co.uk/news/world-news/frances-prime-minister-drenched-sweat-6352891](https://substack.com/redirect/b54dcec1-6159-4d4b-b90e-991a4de66c36?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Perhaps the best way to understand the different perspectives of the elites and the working class is a common refrain popularized during the [Yellow Vest protests](https://substack.com/redirect/20a2b621-919a-48f7-9ce7-ab9a02ddc1a0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) against a carbon tax increase: "They're worried about the end of the world. We're worried about the end of the month."
All of this reminds me of the debate around COVID during the height of the pandemic. Experts, focused on a very narrow problem, advocated a sweeping lockdown that closed schools and shut down the economy. But because they were so narrowly focused on a single problem - stopping the spread of a virus - they didn't consider the wider consequences of the policies they pushed.
What was cost of kids not being in schools? How might constant mask-wearing impact social development for infants learning to communicate via facial reactions? What was the cost of a missed mammogram, or postponed dentist appointment? And what was the cost of people sitting at home, unable to earn a living, support their families, or pay their bills? _While these costs might be unknowable, they certainly weren 't zero_.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..xtrU5a4Y7X70JGAqZSO8q-1drsh9dW_0rDyZoiHzAvg?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=169388892)
Similarly, European elites are experts that are increasingly focused on climate change may be missing the big picture. There are everyday realities that the people they govern face, such as the need to educate their children. I'm not suggesting we shouldn't think about climate change or the environment, but **does it really make sense to pursue uncertain environmental benefits while incurring certain social and human costs?** After all, let's not forget that while Europe is restricting air conditioning and closing schools, [China is building more coal-fired power plants than ever before and isn't committing to reducing carbon emissions until 2026 at the earliest](https://substack.com/redirect/63525d3b-f08d-4509-aca1-bfb206fd83fd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
One of the clearest thinkers on this topic has been Bjorn Lomborg, someone I've had the pleasure of speaking with several times. The video below is from a conversation I had with him about his book _[False Alarm: How Climate Change Panic Costs Us Trillions, Hurts the Poor, and Fails to Fix the Planet](https://substack.com/redirect/6c77d9c0-259b-4456-9dd9-990bee38a060?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/3805acf9-04e3-4096-8632-21d0fc9cbf17?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
In the end, the issue of air conditioning - like so many complex issues these days - comes down to an ever-increasing need for us to take a generalist approach to navigating uncertainty and to think clearly about complicated challenges. It's critical we look through multiple lenses and make decisions with appropriate awareness of the inherent tradeoffs in every decision...because ultimately, leaders who want to stay in power must balance "end of the world" thinking with "end of the month" realities.

---|---|---
**VIKRAM MANSHARAMANI** is an entrepreneur, consultant, scholar, neighbor, husband, father, volunteer, and professional generalist who thinks in multiple-dimensions and looks beyond the short-term. Self-taught to think around corners and connect original dots, he spends his time speaking with global leaders in business, government, academia, and journalism. **He 's currently the Chairman and CEO of Goodwell Foods, a manufacturer of private label frozen pizza**. _LinkedIn_ has twice listed him as its #1 Top Voice in Money & Finance, and _Worth_ profiled him as one of the 100 Most Powerful People in Global Finance. Vikram earned a PhD From MIT, has taught at Yale and Harvard, and is the author of three books, _**[The Making of a Generalist: An Independent Thinker Finds Unconventional Success in an Uncertain World](https://substack.com/redirect/b821f90e-33ba-4c58-81f0-2fffa6630128?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_ , _**[Think for Yourself: Restoring Common Sense in an Age of Experts and Artificial Intelligence](https://substack.com/redirect/3b3e1ca3-ee5b-4dd7-ac8a-3308f5d2dc59?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_ and _**[Boombustology: Spotting Financial Bubbles Before They Burst](https://substack.com/redirect/7d400a88-206f-448e-942d-473d67b33893?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_. Vikram lives in Lincoln, New Hampshire with his wife and two children, where they can usually be found hiking or skiing.
You're currently a free subscriber to [Navigating Uncertainty](https://substack.com/redirect/8eee5915-2b26-4454-8b98-6526166ee77c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). clean_body: "The End of the Month" versus "The End of the World" | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more ? "The End of the Month" versus "The End of the World" | | | Jul 27 | | | | | | | | | | | | | | ---|---|--- In my home state of New Hampshire, July can get hot. While it's perhaps not as frequent as you might experience in Louisiana, there are lots of days we suffer a wet, sticky kind of hot that makes your clothes stick to you and makes it difficult to breathe. And as anyone from Las Vegas can attest, while heat can be uncomfortable, heat and humidity can be unbearable. And it's this humid heat that contributed to my recent decision to install a nitrogen tunnel at the pizza factory I now run. By spraying liquid nitrogen, we're able to rapidly cool hot products - a functionality that enhances food safety, food quality, and our ability to operate during hot, humid New Hampshire summer days. This has proven especially important (and useful) over the past month when the temperature on the factory floor has soared on several days. If we didn't have the ability to use liquid nitrogen, we would likely have been forced to shut down production on those 90 degree plus days. | | | | ---|---|--- | Jun 14 | All of this got me thinking about air conditioning, and in particular the differing attitudes held by Americans and non-Americans about our use of energy to cool our living spaces. In India, where rolling blackouts led to on again, off again air conditioning, I recall watching swings of almost 35 degrees on the thermostat one August almost a decade ago. I've also found myself struggling to sleep in emerging market countries as varied as Ghana, Malaysia, Uruguay, and Pakistan. These travels always made me feel grateful to live in a country that had regular electricity and could afford air conditioning. Even if money couldn't buy happiness, it could buy comfort...or so I believed. Turns out I was wrong; it 's not just about money. The politics of air conditioning are heating up and nowhere is this dynamic more on display than in Europe, a part of the world that should be able to afford comfort. While a recent heat wave is the nominal cause, there are deeper tensions to consider, as noted in a recent Wall Street Journal . Air conditioning has emerged as a political football in the scrum between European conservatives and liberals. In the United Kingdom, conservatives called for the easing of regulations that make it hard for air conditioning to be installed in new housing; and in Spain, the right-wing Vox party has been attacking the country's mainstream parties as failing to protect people from the heat. And in France, populist Marine Le Pen has called for an and apparently problematic conditions in nursing homes. All of this had generated political backlash, mainly from those in power and environmentalists. The that, in France, "Energy minister Agnes Pannier-Runacher said large-scale air conditioning would heat up streets with the machines' exhaust, making heat waves worse" and went on to note she was "flanked by the perspiring prime minister, François Bayrou." (Note: sweating French Prime Ministers are not a new phenomenon in France, see picture below, taken in 2015). The French media also joined the fight against air conditioning, warning that going from an air-conditioned room to the hot outdoors could trigger "thermal shock, resulting in nausea, loss of consciousness, and even respiratory arrest." | | ---|---|--- Source: Perhaps the best way to understand the different perspectives of the elites and the working class is a common refrain popularized during the against a carbon tax increase: "They're worried about the end of the world. Voter Action Project [https://secure.everyaction.com/Qyv_-_BiF0iduOUt5sZ0yw2?am=20.00&contactdata=6a8bPWJA8Xdp1dGAwY70UuflDB8FuJy+p55Qy+ibuJYlWt1fQqmxTORrS1lOqFgwKaFG4Ck9NF+iDjWCw8TEUxA27Jl2WGnhq4lpXlZylIk=]
You are invited to join our one-click issue response poll.
For data integrity purposes, please do not complete this survey if your email address is not dpwillis67@gmail.com .
Team: Attorney General Pam Bondi has been in office roughly six months, and we want to know how voters feel about her job performance.
Bondi has a long history covering up for Donald Trump. As Florida's AG, she examined allegations against Trump University, but then suddenly stopped the nascent investigation after Donald Trump made a $25,000 donation to her campaign fund. After Bondi ended her term as Florida AG, she became one of Trump's chief election deniers, spreading the false claim that the 2020 election had been stolen and refusing to answer direct questions about who won the 2020 election, more than four years after the fact.
Bondi has called for labeling people who commit vandalism against a private company (Tesla) as "domestic terrorists". Now, Bondi is allegedly suppressing the Epstein files and negotiating with convicted Epstein accomplice Ghislaine Maxwell for a supposed immunity deal, with allegations that Bondi is trying to cover up Trump's alleged involvement with Epstein's underage sex ring.
We're looking to see how our supporters feel about her job performance. Please take our quick survey:
Team: Do you approve of the job Pam Bondi is doing as AG?
By clicking a response below your answer will be automatically recorded:

Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.

subject: Records show you’ve not taken our poll >>>
disclaimer_text: Paid for by VPP
body:
We need your response before midnight >>
| **We told you:** Donald Trump continues to ATTACK our free press — _including PBS, NPR, and late night host Stephen Colbert!_

**We told you:** Democrats ARE STANDING WITH Colbert, and are even supporting him MOVING to MSNBC! [](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/colbert-msnbc/?source=VPP_20250727_EMPOLL_E3_T0_NS&t=6&akid=20744%2E878179%2E07Qnyo) **But sadly, you and 14 fellow Democrats from your area did not respond to our poll if you’d watch Stephen Colbert on MSNBC...** [**We’ve extended our poll ONE LAST TIME so 15 Democrats can respond URGENTLY. Please, friend: take our Stephen Colbert on MSNBC Poll to help us stand with journalists and our free press — NOT MAGA extremists like Donald Trump. This poll will close at 10:15 P.M., so TAKE ACTION BEFORE IT’S TOO LATE »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/colbert-msnbc/?source=VPP_20250727_EMPOLL_E3_T0_NS&t=7&akid=20744%2E878179%2E07Qnyo) | [TAKE OUR POLL: SHOULD STEPHEN COLBERT MOVE TO MSNBC?](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/colbert-msnbc/?source=VPP_20250727_EMPOLL_E3_T0_NS&t=8&akid=20744%2E878179%2E07Qnyo)
---
— The Voter Participation Project
— FORWARDED MESSAGE —
**YOU HAVE BEEN SELECTED TO PARTICIPATE IN OUR TIME-SENSITIVE FLASH POLL!**
* * *
_**2,000 DEMOCRATIC RESPONSES NEEDED from your area!**_ [](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/colbert-msnbc/?source=VPP_20250727_EMPOLL_E3_T0_NS&t=9&akid=20744%2E878179%2E07Qnyo) | [YES, I WOULD »](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/colbert-msnbc/?source=VPP_20250727_EMPOLL_E3_T0_NS&t=10&akid=20744%2E878179%2E07Qnyo)
---
[NO, I WOULD NOT! »](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/colbert-msnbc/?source=VPP_20250727_EMPOLL_E3_T0_NS&t=11&akid=20744%2E878179%2E07Qnyo)
**ICYMI:** Late night host STEPHEN COLBERT just found out his show got canceled, and Trump is _EXCITED!_
[](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/colbert-msnbc/?source=VPP_20250727_EMPOLL_E3_T0_NS&t=12&akid=20744%2E878179%2E07Qnyo)
Because of Trump’s lawsuit against CBS, Stephen Colbert will no longer get to report on this administration’s cruel policies. **This is a HUGE BLOW to our free press, and Colbert’s firing should SCARE US ALL!**

Night after night, Colbert’s reporting on MAGA’s far-right agenda gave MILLIONS access to the TRUTH about Donald Trump. **But as a result, Colbert is facing ATTACKS from Trump and extremist Republicans.** _They’re determined to defame and plummet his ratings even further before he goes off the air._

**But if Stephen Colbert moves to MSNBC,** he can continue his reporting and EXPOSE Trump’s dangerous lies — _this would INFURIATE TRUMP!_

We need to hear feedback from Top Democrats in your area:
[**Respond by Midnight: Would you watch Stephen Colbert if he moved to MSNBC?! »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/colbert-msnbc/?source=VPP_20250727_EMPOLL_E3_T0_NS&t=13&akid=20744%2E878179%2E07Qnyo)
[YES, I WOULD »](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/colbert-msnbc/?source=VPP_20250727_EMPOLL_E3_T0_NS&t=14&akid=20744%2E878179%2E07Qnyo)
---
[NO, I WOULD NOT »](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/colbert-msnbc/?source=VPP_20250727_EMPOLL_E3_T0_NS&t=15&akid=20744%2E878179%2E07Qnyo)
— Research Team at The Voter Protection Project
| | Paid for by VPP
(www.protectvoting.org)
Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. 401 2nd Ave. S, Ste. 303, Seattle, WA 98104

The Voter Protection Project is on an important mission: To end partisan gerrymandering and voter suppression by electing candidates at the state and federal levels that are forceful advocates for voting rights. Our enfranchisement agenda includes working to ensure that every state has automatic voter registration and online voter registration, convenient early voting, and independent redistricting commissions to ensure politicians are not able to draw their own maps.

Please, consider making a [one-time contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/121?t=1&akid=20744%2E878179%2E07Qnyo). **Or, to make the most powerful lasting impact and help us oust Republicans up and down the ballot who attack our right to vote, make a[monthly sustaining contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/122?t=2&akid=20744%2E878179%2E07Qnyo).**

[Click here](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/fewer-emails/?t=3&akid=20744%2E878179%2E07Qnyo) to request fewer emails. Click here to [unsubscribe](https://act.protectvoting.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=16&akid=20744%2E878179%2E07Qnyo). | | [Please: Pitch In Once A Month To Protect Voting Rights »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/123?t=4&akid=20744%2E878179%2E07Qnyo)
---
[Donate Just One Time »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/124?t=5&akid=20744%2E878179%2E07Qnyo)

name: Tim Kaine
subject: This race is going to be close
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025-jones [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025-jones]
Friends, I want to talk to you about our great Democratic nominee for Attorney General, Jay Jones.
Jay is a former Virginia lawmaker and attorney who’s running to protect VA families from crime, corporate overreach and fraud, and extreme agendas that put our rights at risk.
He comes from a long legacy of fighting for Virginians. Jay is the grandson of a civil rights attorney who was the first Black person to be appointed to the Virginia Board of Education in the history of the Commonwealth and the son of a longtime VA state delegate and a family court judge. And like his family, he’s committed to ensuring Virginia is a place where everyone can succeed.
But Jay will be facing a tough battle this November against incumbent Attorney General Jason Miyares.
Miyares is a rubber-stamp for Trump’s MAGA agenda, including the devastating mass firing of federal workers that had serious impacts on Virginia’s huge federal workforce.
What’s more, a Trump-backed super PAC has just invested a six-figure sum in advertising supporting Miyares.
So Jay Jones needs our help. I’m proud to support him, and I’m asking you to join me today: Will you pitch in any amount to Jay Jones and my Common Ground PAC today? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025-jones]
If you’ve saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
DONATE $20 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025-jones?express_lane=true&amount=20]
DONATE $50 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025-jones?express_lane=true&amount=50]
DONATE $100 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025-jones?express_lane=true&amount=100]
DONATE OTHER [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025-jones]
No matter how much you give, I promise your contributions go a long way in our fight to flip Virginia’s three statewide offices blue and elect Democrats all the way down the ticket this November.
Thank you so much for your support.
-Tim Kaine
Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
1490-5A Quarterpath Road #272
Williamsburg, VA 23185
Emails like this one are a critical part of staying in touch during this important election year. We value your support, and are so glad to have you on the team. If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/P5FfEbyeIEejNQSsVSzanQ2?unsubscribedata=FvMpsahexGCC8KLG9wjA2W4xq7zpL124YSSwQ8T8kmyjb%2B6Rg2GfTTKgE5EyfqzDahBzuX%2FF3ZuI6qhkBIyxqOI7A%2F%2BTHcKuBV0OJ0cjaHGi1CzkQVNN9mX%2B4uYjGh2o%2FPIxlwvmk0tmZqaaLKaoNe5UoNLVdtipEFEc1sXs2GdpQL%2F18vC%2FWjC%2F%2FywvZgNhYXSUcc7CgEB%2FCg1V5LIGy2yD2siCKpQSNH5FFUNn9OFH64sy1JYZLFMySpFpBVq9LC0R6VXd7H9rc%2F9Nr5CUdSqVugwc8Be5E6cElfLaFhumqGno60YcSHj5UV%2B3u6Njy8%2FV5DHqLIOZC5I30Snh0AFGXBj0OrJ9aisIn6naTCU483%2FNG2UpWXd78arX0AMkjBcGi8840K97dM4od8KeqNAEFpIRKCCtWpt37d%2B65rI%3D .

What’s more, a Trump-backed super PAC has just invested a six-figure sum in advertising supporting Miyares. So Jay Jones needs our help. I’m proud to support him, and I’m asking you to join me today: Will you pitch in any amount to Jay Jones and my Common Ground PAC today? [] If you’ve saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: DONATE $20 [] DONATE $50 [] DONATE $100 [] DONATE OTHER [] No matter how much you give, I promise your contributions go a long way in our fight to flip Virginia’s three statewide offices blue and elect Democrats all the way down the ticket this November. Thank you so much for your support. -Tim Kaine Paid for by Kaine for Virginia 1490-5A Quarterpath Road #272 Williamsburg, VA 23185 Emails like this one are a critical part of staying in touch during this important election year. We value your support, and are so glad to have you on the team. If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: .

|

| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/112240391/559866236/172610512?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_FR_EOM1_250727_T1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlM2UzZGVmNS0zMTZiLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=BvM9CoAq1ABCZs4YS6hejLacRhhSpQ1uuWr5g7Z0igE=) Derek,

If the past 189 days of the second Trump administration have shown us anything, it’s that no matter how insane, chaotic, or dangerous Trump is, my Republican colleagues are _still_ more than willing to bend the knee and do whatever he says.

I want to explain what this means for my fight to hold Trump accountable, but first, [_**I’m asking if you can pitch in before our July fundraising deadline. We’ve set a goal of raising an additional $50,000 before the month ends, and we’re counting on you to help us reach it >>**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/112240392/559866237/172610512?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_FR_EOM1_250727_T1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlM2UzZGVmNS0zMTZiLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=BvM9CoAq1ABCZs4YS6hejLacRhhSpQ1uuWr5g7Z0igE=)

Let’s look at the facts: Trump’s Big, Ugly Bill? Republicans _knew_ it would rip millions off their health insurance, raise prices, and explode our national debt – just to give tax cuts to the wealthiest Americans. My Republican colleagues _knew_ it was bad legislation. But they voted for it anyway.

When Elon Musk and his DOGE bros took a chainsaw to the federal government? The GOP stood back in silence and watched hardworking public servants get fired over Trump’s politically-motivated vendetta.

And don’t even get me started on the Epstein files... Republicans are allowing Trump to peddle ridiculous conspiracy theories to distract Americans from the truth.

Look, I’ve tried to work across the aisle to get enough Republicans on our side to put a real check on this administration’s worst impulses. But here’s the reality: **taking back the Senate for Democrats is the only way we can put a check on Trump.**

The problem is, if we lose Virginia next year, Democrats have no chance of taking back the Senate. And now, I’m reading reports that a Trump-aligned super PAC has raised a record-breaking $85 million just this year to go after Democrat incumbents like me and prop up Trump’s candidates.

[_**That’s why I’m asking if you’ll chip in to my reelection campaign before our end-of-month fundraising deadline. We need to raise $50,000 to meet our fundraising benchmarks for the month so we can fight off the attacks we know are coming my way, win this race, and take back the Senate for Democrats.**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/112240393/559866238/172610512?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_FR_EOM1_250727_T1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlM2UzZGVmNS0zMTZiLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=BvM9CoAq1ABCZs4YS6hejLacRhhSpQ1uuWr5g7Z0igE=) name: Rep. Maxwell Frost
subject: Republicans still aren’t showing up, so we’re pulling up.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY MAXWELL FROST FOR CONGRESS
body:
| [ ](http://clicksp.frostforcongress.com/f/a/HLqeOikfDOG2azv3oLm_zQ~~/AAPPABA~/GTPUROrrfUPhWiKr7Cm3Bq2CuXsCdSdqsgPnshNiEcSDMl188H1ypthniFi4Wftg4UTPzX9Sw9sQR0OM7metWaPM0-UqIc_6MO8EfJ3dfhYfXXsfMgO81wzNXrDShf4p0uMcNeRdLMwz7SsnkoTHzb_UiS2M0DeSzQGF1NgayZx1HJPu0dx0u20HK8sR1Myae8sLrmcBqhOkPx2yLAty-aBfTc1RDyVorXGTFP3y6SDpDIg9n6TtrEfJy4vHExT1MgD_oX83UI7bF726IpwrTXZFRXliDV6lhJ9q8HCJrQfj7u6KSi-D9h_IK2GnHuYgrKeeFCXylddxjNwAzfFXVzn0R39VrUjGBNdcv1EU3R8~)
---
| Yesterday, Sen. Chris Murphy and I packed the house for a town hall in Dayton, Ohio - a district where Republican Rep. Mike Turner has REFUSED to show up for his constituents.

But while Rep. Turner was still in hiding, HUNDREDS of his voters turned out.
[ ](http://clicksp.frostforcongress.com/f/a/HLqeOikfDOG2azv3oLm_zQ~~/AAPPABA~/GTPUROrrfUPhWiKr7Cm3Bq2CuXsCdSdqsgPnshNiEcSDMl188H1ypthniFi4Wftg4UTPzX9Sw9sQR0OM7metWaPM0-UqIc_6MO8EfJ3dfhYfXXsfMgO81wzNXrDShf4p0uMcNeRdLMwz7SsnkoTHzb_UiS2M0DeSzQGF1NgayZx1HJPu0dx0u20HK8sR1Myae8sLrmcBqhOkPx2yLAty-aBfTc1RDyVorXGTFP3y6SDpDIg9n6TtrEfJy4vHExT1MgD_oX83UI7bF726IpwrTXZFRXliDV6lhJ9q8HCJrQfj7u6KSi-D9h_IK2GnHuYgrKeeFCXylddxjNwAzfFXVzn0R39VrUjGBNdcv1EU3R8~) It’s undeniable: working folks are fed up, and they should be. While people struggle just to afford the basics, Republicans are casting votes that could sabotage their livelihoods.

But the people aren’t backing down – they’re standing up, getting louder, and demanding accountability.

Because you and I both know that it shouldn’t matter if your member of Congress is a Republican or Democrat. They should give a damn about you.

**Well, I give a damn.** That’s why I’m holding town halls in red districts across the country – because while Republicans hide and vote to cut SNAP and Medicaid, working people are coming together. And thanks to you, I’m right there with them.

But holding town halls across the country takes a lot of planning, and a lot of resources. That’s why I’m counting on your support so we can stay on the road and bring even more people into this fight for our future.

[**_Just one donation from you today will keep me on the road so we can grow our people-powered movement against Republicans’ attacks on working people. Can you give just $1 as your first donation to my campaign so I can hold even more red-district town halls across the country? >>_**](http://clicksp.frostforcongress.com/f/a/6rvIqr3NT2_CM4m95H7Reg~~/AAPPABA~/ezGM2esKDFgj3WHOw3ZSeuHVcQkQHXkqsviUkr2nZ08zoHWwy7-CLxew8SGteGISTJVFxdmnQ0An_eQSmpGtrzmAvJ4qRAivd_Rb1Sm9U6NjDRJ1_K6bnpmRnzN4Tp3vGhuZIZG1sY0uNpzXKvg8Ir5zIVQCDv1PZHkX6_0CRNJe3QwY2poi2jwPoJM4ZnAFm5_Uc2NU52JeYjYAlc7bGrO_JVucqnEt9OX1xs8QmRkHSmelYHsbjJRHUUdal4XYYuogIwYMYcOeFNZgihWs2PCWe7QZrhlv9PaRbtsSV3iTcXKJjcJzt8Zx4oahETXSkuyN0JAKgE7u83-IMoM4yg~~)

name: Attention Needed
subject: Before you log off >
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Josh Weil, Democrat for US Senate
body:
[https://d15k2d11r6t6rl.cloudfront.net/public/users/Integrators/04eaa6b2-88f4-43b4-b978-7bb0708f5838/387153444E713877456653376A556574376F314841345859327A4D665362374C42586753794B64635252546F6551413647436E7356536243345157355952664A/editor_images/Screenshot%202025-06-18%20at%2011.41.25%E2%80%AFAM.png]
Friend, Before you close your laptop, silence your phone, or head to dinner, I just want to ask one thing:
Can you take a moment to save lives?
I am Josh Weil, running for U.S. Senate. I am running to restore affordability and ensure the safety of every American. This campaign isn’t backed by billionaires. It’s powered by people who know Florida deserves better, people who want to fix a system that’s been rigged by career politicians and corporate donors.
Taking a second to help us hit our goals gets thousands re-registered to vote and connect, and thousands more saved from horrible, predatory policy.

Since launching this campaign, I have visited communities all across the Commonwealth — from Farmville to Fairfax, Richmond to Roanoke — to hear directly from Virginians about the challenges they face.
_And that's informed what we communicate and how we spend our resources._
My opponent, Winsome Earle-Sears, has shown she's in lockstep with Trump's agenda. She downplayed the White House laying off thousands of Virginia workers by saying, "don't fret." And she boasted that Trump's disastrous tax law does "so many great things." _We're talking about a policy that cuts $1 trillion from Medicaid and slashes resources for public schools here._
I've given voters my word: **As Governor, I will always put them first and stand up to the chaos coming out of Washington.**
We can't be defined by what we're against — we must focus on moving our Commonwealth forward.
That's why I have announced my plans to grow our workforce and raise the minimum wage, to reduce the cost of childcare, and to make life more affordable from healthcare and prescription drugs to housing costs and utility bills.
With the remaining 100 days left in this campaign, I'm counting on a surge of grassroots donations to:
✅ Visit every corner of Virginia.
✅ Reach undecided voters with ads and counter the GOP's attacks.
✅ Build the largest organizing operation our Commonwealth's ever seen.
It's a major undertaking, but I know this team is not intimidated by hard work. No matter what National Republicans throw at us, we'll be ready to respond.
[_**Everything depends on us raising what we need from grassroots donors. So, I'm asking: will you pitch in $25 or whatever you can manage right now to help us run a campaign that will win on November 4th?**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/112241164/559869089/830632682?refcode=BLU_EM_ASFG_2025-07-27_2&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS8xMDM4NTgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNDM2YmI4MzYtMzc2Yi1mMDExLThkYzktNjA0NWJkYTlkOTZiIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=bhpwIw-FoF6pdAX2WrD0uEDiXBCj7-9BcySJP-2kZpg=)

clean_body: We have to make each day left in this campaign count. = Peter, we have 100 days to go until Election Day! 100 days. I'm the only Democrat in the country who can flip a Governor's seat blue, and we're in the homestretch now. = If you are signed up for ActBlue Express, your contribution to Abigail Spanberger will be processed immediately: = = = = Since launching this campaign, I have visited communities all across the Commonwealth — from Farmville to Fairfax, Richmond to Roanoke — to hear directly from Virginians about the challenges they face. And that's informed what we communicate and how we spend our resources. My opponent, Winsome Earle-Sears, has shown she's in lockstep with Trump's agenda. She downplayed the White House laying off thousands of Virginia workers by saying, "don't fret." And she boasted that Trump's disastrous tax law does "so many great things." We're talking about a policy that cuts $1 trillion from Medicaid and slashes resources for public schools here. I've given voters my word: As Governor, I will always put them first and stand up to the chaos coming out of Washington. We can't be defined by what we're against — we must focus on moving our Commonwealth forward. That's why I have announced my plans to grow our workforce and raise the minimum wage, to reduce the cost of childcare, and to make life more affordable from healthcare and prescription drugs to housing costs and utility bills. With the remaining 100 days left in this campaign, I'm counting on a surge of grassroots donations to: ✅ Visit every corner of Virginia. ✅ Reach undecided voters with ads and counter the GOP's attacks. ✅ Build the largest organizing operation our Commonwealth's ever seen. It's a major undertaking, but I know this team is not intimidated by hard work. No matter what National Republicans throw at us, we'll be ready to respond. = If you are signed up for ActBlue Express, your contribution to Abigail Spanberger will be processed immediately: = = = = = = I'm grateful to have you on my team, Abigail Spanberger By submitting your phone number you're agreeing to receive periodic text messages from the Spanberger for Governor campaign. name: John Hickenlooper
subject: I never thought I'd run for office
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY HICKENLOOPER FOR COLORADO
body:
I'm just a geologist-turned-brewer who entered public service to do what's right for the people of my state.

[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/112241185/559869143/1435897075?refcode=email_072725_bio-RS&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83MjY1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhYjI5NWQ4Yy0zNzZiLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=dUPyT3Z2HwmIQWom3L72nv7jWMox0ruDhKHRdVrsOrU=&emci=c1e2145e-8769-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&emdi=ab295d8c-376b-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&ceid=7280715) I'll be honest, folks. I never saw myself serving in the U.S. Senate.

This is a longer email about my story and how I came to fight for hardworking Coloradans in the Senate, and I hope you’ll read it until the end. **[But if you don’t have time, please, take a moment and chip in a couple bucks to my campaign before time runs out on our end-of-month deadline. →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/112241186/559869144/1973964750?refcode=email_072725_bio-RS&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83MjY1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhYjI5NWQ4Yy0zNzZiLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=dUPyT3Z2HwmIQWom3L72nv7jWMox0ruDhKHRdVrsOrU=)** | | [Donate →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/112241187/559869145/1973964750?refcode=email_072725_bio-RS&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83MjY1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhYjI5NWQ4Yy0zNzZiLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=dUPyT3Z2HwmIQWom3L72nv7jWMox0ruDhKHRdVrsOrU=)
---
Back in the mid-1980s, I was a geologist – until I was laid off for 27 months. Then, I took every scrap of savings in my bank account and took a gamble on opening my own business, Wynkoop Brewing Company, in downtown Denver.
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/112241188/559869146/1973964750?refcode=email_072725_bio-RS&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83MjY1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhYjI5NWQ4Yy0zNzZiLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=dUPyT3Z2HwmIQWom3L72nv7jWMox0ruDhKHRdVrsOrU=)
I remember working 80-hour weeks. I remember lying awake at night wondering how we were going to make it. And I remember being so angry that my government wasn't working for me.

So, I ran for Mayor. And once elected, I focused every day on making decisions based on what was best for Denver.

Then, I was elected Governor on the premise of uniting people, finding common ground, hashing things out, and getting things done.

And after we flipped Colorado’s Senate seat in 2020, I got my hands dirty working hard to deliver real change for Coloradans. We invested billions of dollars to confront the climate crisis, lowered prescription drug costs, made investments to rebuild our infrastructure, and brought thousands of good-paying manufacturing jobs back to America.

But look, I’ve also seen how broken Washington is. I see politicians who only listen to those who cut big campaign checks while ignoring the rest of us. And it’s clear we need more leaders who are willing to cut through the political deadlock and work to move this country forward.

Here's why I'm telling you this: **my story is the reason I remain grounded amid the nonsense and dysfunction that's happening in our government.**

Because with all the chaos in Washington, you need somebody who rolls up their sleeves and fights every day for all of us – not just those they agree with.

Like I said, I never thought I'd find myself in the U.S. Senate, but I’m running for re-election because Coloradans deserve a leader they can trust to do right by them. And I'm just a geologist-turned-brewer who entered public service to do what's right for the people of my state.

That’s why I don't take money from corporate PACs like your typical politician. Instead, I rely on grassroots folks like you. And it’s why I'm depending on your contribution today to help us meet our end-of-month fundraising goa. Even just $5 here or $10 there goes a long way in a campaign like ours.
**[So I have to ask: will you make a donation of whatever is meaningful to you before our deadline expires? Together, we can defend this seat we flipped back in 2020 and keep power in the hands of Coloradans:](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/112241189/559869147/1973964750?refcode=email_072725_bio-RS&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83MjY1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhYjI5NWQ4Yy0zNzZiLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=dUPyT3Z2HwmIQWom3L72nv7jWMox0ruDhKHRdVrsOrU=)**
_If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_

clean_body: I'm just a geologist-turned-brewer who entered public service to do what's right for the people of my state. I'll be honest, folks. I never saw myself serving in the U.S. Senate. This is a longer email about my story and how I came to fight for hardworking Coloradans in the Senate, and I hope you’ll read it until the end. = | | = Back in the mid-1980s, I was a geologist – until I was laid off for 27 months. Then, I took every scrap of savings in my bank account and took a gamble on opening my own business, Wynkoop Brewing Company, in downtown Denver. = I remember working 80-hour weeks. I remember lying awake at night wondering how we were going to make it. And I remember being so angry that my government wasn't working for me. So, I ran for Mayor. And once elected, I focused every day on making decisions based on what was best for Denver. Then, I was elected Governor on the premise of uniting people, finding common ground, hashing things out, and getting things done. And after we flipped Colorado’s Senate seat in 2020, I got my hands dirty working hard to deliver real change for Coloradans. We invested billions of dollars to confront the climate crisis, lowered prescription drug costs, made investments to rebuild our infrastructure, and brought thousands of good-paying manufacturing jobs back to America. But look, I’ve also seen how broken Washington is. I see politicians who only listen to those who cut big campaign checks while ignoring the rest of us. And it’s clear we need more leaders who are willing to cut through the political deadlock and work to move this country forward. Here's why I'm telling you this: my story is the reason I remain grounded amid the nonsense and dysfunction that's happening in our government. Because with all the chaos in Washington, you need somebody who rolls up their sleeves and fights every day for all of us – not just those they agree with. Like I said, I never thought I'd find myself in the U.S. Senate, but I’m running for re-election because Coloradans deserve a leader they can trust to do right by them. And I'm just a geologist-turned-brewer who entered public service to do what's right for the people of my state. That’s why I don't take money from corporate PACs like your typical politician. Instead, I rely on grassroots folks like you. And it’s why I'm depending on your contribution today to help us meet our end-of-month fundraising goa. Even just $5 here or $10 there goes a long way in a campaign like ours. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | = | = | | | = | = | | | = | = Thanks for all your support, John | Email is a key way for us to stay in touch and make sure you get the latest updates from our campaign. But if you would like to unsubscribe, . | PAID FOR BY HICKENLOOPER FOR COLORADO Hickenlooper for Colorado PO Box 300007 Denver, CO 80203 United States

name: GOTV PAC
subject: Trump’s FDA is pushing junk science. Here’s how we stop it:
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY GET OUT THE VOTE PAC NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE OR ANY CANDIDATE'S COMMITTEE
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpacemq2?refcode=Email06302025&amount=25 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpacemq2?refcode=Email06302025&amount=25]
Peter ,
Under Donald Trump and RFK Jr., the FDA just gave a microphone to anti-vaxxers, YouTubers, and conspiracy theorists to attack antidepressants and question whether depression is even real.
Let that sink in.
Instead of amplifying the overwhelming scientific consensus on mental health treatment, they platformed misinformation. One panelist said SSRIs (Selective Serotonin Reuptake Inhibitors) are more dangerous than heroin. Another claimed mental illness is just a sign of "emotional intensity." And the FDA moderator treated these fringe views as equal to real, peer-reviewed science.
This is the Trump playbook: rewrite reality, attack the experts, and turn public institutions into a megaphone for MAGA ideology.
If we don't take back the House in 2026, this is the future:
🛑 Public health shaped by podcasts instead of peer-reviewed science
🛑 Medical policy driven by paranoia, not data
🛑 A government that treats mental illness like a moral failure, not a medical condition
GOTV PAC is organizing to elect leaders who believe in facts , science , and basic human decency. But with Trump's allies already gearing up, we need your help to fight back NOW. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpacemq2?refcode=Email07272025&amount=25]

Let's make sure voters know the truth—and let's take back the House while we still can.
-GOTV PAC

---|---
We’ve been inundated by a steady stream of destructive policies coming from the Trump administration. If there is an issue or policy you don’t like about the Trump administration, odds are it was one of Stephen Miller’s ideas. He’s considered one of Trump’s most powerful advisors.
He’s been known to espouse white nationalist beliefs and has promoted far-right conspiracy theories, _and he has the ear of Donald Trump._
The good news is that we’re not powerless. We’ve already made incredible strides in the states to combat the radical and dangerous Trump-Miller agenda. States still have an incredible ability to blunt the impacts of Trump and Miller’s actions on areas like health care access and education.
**Our state legislatures are our strongest firewalls – and the DLCC is the committee best equipped to fight back. We’re fast approaching Thursday’s midnight deadline, and falling short could mean ceding ground to state-level Republicans closely aligned with Stephen Miller.**

name: Stacey Abrams
subject: Urgent: MTG, DOGE, and the House GOP launched a coordinated attack against my work
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Ohio House Democratic Campaign Committee, 545 E Town St
body:
Here’s what you need to know:

|
---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/112241260/559869479/-1847388832?refcode=250727_Abrams_DOGE&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NDI2NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1YTFlYWFlZi0zODZiLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=lxtULvwGXfX1Ed8vU1UzDOa-xyLjrr97WuALrQepI9Y=) Folks, **MTG, DOGE, and House Republicans launched an all-out attack on me in a committee hearing.** Here’s what you need to know: → For years, I’ve worked alongside dozens of leaders to turn Georgia into a battleground, empowering new voters. → Now, Georgia is purple, and the GOP is terrified. They’re launching baseless accusations to tear me down and undo all the progress we’ve made. → This attack isn’t just about me — it’s the start of a coordinated Republican plan to take down leaders across the country who are fighting to make a difference and strengthen the right to vote. And Ohio House Dems are also under fire for their work to fight back. But we’re not backing down. We won’t let them stop us, and we need you with us. [**Will you split a donation between Ohio House Dems and Speak Up PAC today? clean_body: Here’s what you need to know: | = Folks, MTG, DOGE, and House Republicans launched an all-out attack on me in a committee hearing. Here’s what you need to know: → For years, I’ve worked alongside dozens of leaders to turn Georgia into a battleground, empowering new voters. → Now, Georgia is purple, and the GOP is terrified. They’re launching baseless accusations to tear me down and undo all the progress we’ve made. → This attack isn’t just about me — it’s the start of a coordinated Republican plan to take down leaders across the country who are fighting to make a difference and strengthen the right to vote. And Ohio House Dems are also under fire for their work to fight back. But we’re not backing down. We won’t let them stop us, and we need you with us. = | | = "" | = "" | = "" | = "" Let’s make sure they don’t succeed. -Stacey Abrams = = = OHDC 545 E.
